Just hours before the official announcement, Nikam received a phone call from Prime Minister Narendra Modi, who personally informed him of the nomination. The Prime Minister humorously asked whether he should speak in Hindi or Marathi, and eventually chose Marathi, reflecting a warm and respectful interaction between the two. Nikam shared with ANI that during the call, PM Modi conveyed the President’s decision to entrust him with this responsibility. He expressed his immediate acceptance of the nomination, underscoring the significance of the moment. This nomination is part of a group of four distinguished individuals appointed to the Rajya Sabha, including former diplomat Harsh Vardhan Shringla, historian Meenakshi Jain, and teacher-politician C. Sadanandan Master. This development highlights the recognition of Nikam’s contributions to the country, particularly in the legal domain, as he prepares to take on his new role in the upper house of Parliament.

title: Chinese mother sells sons for $11,600, spends entire amount on livestream hosts and shopping.
content: A woman in China was sentenced to over five years in prison after selling her two biological sons to fund her addiction to tipping livestream hosts and purchasing luxury goods, according to the South China Morning Post (SCMP). The 26-year-old woman, surnamed Huang, hails from Guangxi province in southern China. With only a primary school education and lacking a stable support network, she relocated to Fuzhou in Fujian province and took up various odd jobs to make ends meet.In October 2020, Huang gave birth to her first son but, due to financial difficulties and the absence of the child's father, decided to sell him. Her landlord, surnamed Wei, connected her with a relative, surnamed Li, whose son was infertile and seeking to adopt. The child was sold for 45,000 yuan (approximately US$6,300), and Huang spent the entire sum on livestream tipping. When the funds were depleted, Huang resorted to even more drastic measures. In 2022, she deliberately conceived a second child, named Guyu, with the intention to sell him as well. This second son was sold to a broker for 38,000 yuan (US$5,300), who later sold the baby for 103,000 yuan (US$14,000). Huang again used all the money on extravagant spending, including online tips and clothing. Authorities were alerted to Huang’s activities on April 13, 2022. During the investigation, police discovered chat records on her phone detailing negotiations related to child trafficking. Both children were rescued and are currently under the care of local civil affairs departments, awaiting adoption. On July 8, the Fuzhou Jin’an District People’s Court sentenced Huang to five years and two months in prison for fraud and child trafficking, alongside a fine of 30,000 yuan (US$4,000). Li received a suspended nine-month sentence for purchasing a trafficked child, while Wei was sentenced to seven months in prison.

title: Shubman Gill Compared to Former Captain Virat Kohli; Kumble Responds: ‘England Didn’t Want to Face Just One Player.
content: During the India vs England third Test at Lord's, tensions flared in the final moments of Day 3 as India captain Shubman Gill grew visibly frustrated with England opener Zak Crawley’s time-wasting tactics. With only six minutes remaining, Crawley delayed the game by taking an extended time to take guard and twice halting bowler Jasprit Bumrah’s run-up, which provoked anger among the Indian players. Gill confronted Crawley from the slip cordon, using strong language and gestures to express his displeasure. The situation escalated when Crawley called for the physio in an apparent attempt to waste time, despite there being just enough time for one more delivery before stumps. In response, Gill made a hand signal suggesting Crawley be substituted if genuinely injured. This led to a heated exchange between Crawley and Gill, with Crawley receiving support from his opening partner Ben Duckett. The confrontation ended with both sides standing face-to-face, while India’s KL Rahul attempted to de-escalate the situation. This incident was arguably a culmination of earlier delays during the match, as umpires had permitted several unscheduled breaks and other interruptions during India’s innings. The mounting frustration on both sides highlighted the tension surrounding the match’s progress and England’s efforts to slow the game as the day drew to a close.

title: Bihar Man Allegedly Throws 6-Year-Old Son into Hotel Room in Patna, Child Dies.
content: A tragic incident unfolded near Patna railway station, where a six-year-old boy named Sunny, son of Prabhakar Mahoto, died after allegedly being beaten by his father inside a hotel room. The police were informed early Sunday morning and rushed the child to a nearby hospital, but unfortunately, Sunny succumbed to his injuries. According to Sub-Divisional Police Officer (SDPO) Abhinav, the boy’s mother reported that a domestic dispute with her husband escalated on Saturday night. During the altercation, the father reportedly assaulted Sunny and threw him onto the hotel room floor. Following the incident, the father fled the scene and is currently absconding. Authorities have registered a case and are actively investigating the matter. Efforts are underway to locate the father and bring him to justice, while officials continue to gather details surrounding the tragic event.

title: Veteran Actress B. Saroja Devi, Renowned as Abhinaya Saraswati, Passes Away at 87.
content: Veteran South Indian actress B. Saroja Devi passed away at the age of 87 in Bengaluru on Monday, according to film industry sources. She died at her residence in Malleswaram due to age-related ailments. Saroja Devi, affectionately known as 'Kannadathu Paingili' (Kannada’s parrot) in Tamil, began her illustrious career at the age of 17 with the Kannada film Mahakavi Kalidasa (1955). She earned the distinction of being the first female superstar of Kannada cinema and won a National Award for her debut performance. Her Tamil film Nadodi Mannan (1958), alongside legendary actor and former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.G. Ramachandran, established her as one of Tamil cinema’s leading actresses. Despite marrying in 1967, she remained a prominent figure in the film industry and was fondly referred to as ‘Abhinaya Saraswathi’ for her exceptional acting skills.

title: 19 Years of Golmaal: When Ajay Devgn Embraced Comedy and Never Looked Back.
content: Ajay Devgn’s journey into comedy began with the release of Golmaal on July 14, 2006, a film that redefined his image and showcased his versatility beyond intense and action-packed roles. Nearly two decades later, he continues to embrace comedy, with Son Of Sardaar 2 set to release on July 25, 2025. Golmaal marked a turning point, introducing audiences to a new side of Devgn funny, self-aware, and ready to take on slapstick humor. Before Golmaal, Ajay Devgn was known for his powerful performances in dramatic and action films, captivating viewers with his intense presence. However, his role as Gopal in Golmaal surprised audiences and critics alike, as he effortlessly embraced a comedic persona. His portrayal combined macho charm with humor, delivering memorable scenes that highlighted his timing and wit. Despite being an ensemble comedy, Golmaal became a defining moment for Ajay Devgn’s career, establishing him as a versatile actor capable of both laughs and drama. His iconic "head-bang" gesture, comic exchanges with Arshad Warsi, and impeccable delivery of one-liners endeared him to fans, solidifying his place as a leading comic actor in Bollywood.

title: Karachi-Based Pakistani Drama Group Presents Adaptation of Ramayana, Director Highlights ‘Our Society’s Tolerance.
content: Pakistani drama group Mauj is gaining acclaim for their innovative adaptation of the Ramayana, staged at the Karachi Arts Council. Utilizing AI enhancements, the group has brought new life to the ancient Hindu epic, captivating audiences and critics alike in Karachi, Sindh province. The production has been praised for its engaging storytelling and technical creativity. Director Yoheshwar Karera expressed confidence in the project, stating that he never feared backlash or threats for staging the Ramayana. He emphasized that the performance serves as a testament to the tolerance and cultural openness of Pakistani society, which he believes is often underestimated. The positive reception from both audiences and critics underscores this sentiment. Art and film critic Omair Alavi highlighted the sincerity of the storytelling, noting that elements such as dynamic lighting, live music, vibrant costumes, and evocative stage design contributed significantly to the production’s grandeur. Producer and actor Rana Kazmi, who portrays Sita, shared her enthusiasm for bringing this timeless tale to life, aiming to offer audiences a vivid and immersive experience.

title: Bigg Boss 18’s Kashish Kapoor Registers FIR Against Cook for Alleged Theft of ₹7 Lakh and Assault Claims.
content: Bigg Boss 18 contestant Kashish Kapoor recently alleged that her cook, Sachin Kumar Chaudhary, stole ₹7 lakh in cash from her home. In a detailed video shared on her YouTube channel, Kashish explained how she discovered the theft. She found the envelope containing the money empty when she checked her locker, despite having kept the cash at home with the intention of depositing it into her mother’s account. According to reports, Kashish filed an FIR at the Amboli Police Station in Andheri West, where she resides. Sachin had been working at her home for about five months. Upon realizing the cash was missing, Kashish confronted the cook as he was about to leave. Although he initially denied taking the money, when asked to empty his pockets, a wad of cash fell out. Kashish also claimed that she was assaulted by him during the confrontation.

title: Manoj Bajpayee Announces The Family Man 3 to Premiere This October or November.
content: Fans of The Family Man franchise have long awaited the release date of Season 3, and Manoj Bajpayee has now confirmed that the new season will begin streaming in the last week of October or the first week of November. Bajpayee revealed that shooting is complete and expressed his surprise at the show’s massive success, calling it the most popular project of his career despite having worked on other acclaimed series like Abhishek Chaubey’s Killer Soup. Confident that Season 3 will meet or even exceed expectations, Bajpayee assured fans that those who loved the first two seasons will not be disappointed. While Samantha was the highlight of Season 2, he praised Jaideep Ahlawat as the standout actor of the new season, emphasizing his dedication and commitment to delivering a powerful performance.

title: Aankhon Ki Gustaakhiyan Day 3 Box Office Update: Has Shanaya Kapoor’s Film Reached ₹1 Crore.
content: Bollywood newcomer Shanaya Kapoor’s debut film Aankhon Ki Gustaakhiyan, co-starring Vikrant Massey, has had a modest performance at the box office during its opening weekend. Released on Friday, the romantic drama has earned approximately ₹1.2 crore (India net) over its first three days, reflecting a lukewarm response from audiences. According to Sacnilk data, the film collected ₹0.30 crore on its opening day and ₹0.49 crore on Saturday, showing a slight upward trend. On Sunday, Aankhon Ki Gustaakhiyan added around ₹0.41 crore, bringing its total opening weekend collection to about ₹1.2 crore. Occupancy rates for the film have been moderate, with Sunday seeing a slight improvement at 17.11% overall compared to Saturday's 15.25% in Hindi circuits. Morning shows had the lowest occupancy at 7.40%, while afternoon and evening shows performed better with 20.09% and 23.14% respectively. However, night shows recorded 0% occupancy, likely due to limited or canceled late-night screenings.

title: R Madhavan Addresses Hindi-Marathi Language Debate, States “Language Has Never Been an Issue for Me.
content: Actor R. Madhavan recently shared his perspective on the ongoing Hindi-Marathi language debate, emphasizing that he has never faced any language-related difficulties in India. Drawing from his personal experiences of living across different states and cultures, Madhavan explained that language has never been a barrier in his life or career. Growing up in Jamshedpur and having studied Marathi in Kolhapur, he highlighted his multilingual background, stating that he has always been comfortable communicating in Tamil, Hindi, and Marathi. The controversy gained momentum when the Maharashtra government mandated Hindi as a third language in state-run primary schools, alongside Marathi and English, following the national three-language policy. Tensions escalated after social media circulated videos of MNS workers allegedly targeting non-Marathi speakers, sparking widespread discussions on linguistic and regional identity. Several celebrities have weighed in on the issue, advocating for respect and unity among India’s diverse languages. Singer Udit Narayan, for instance, emphasized the significance of honoring regional languages like Marathi while also recognizing the equal importance of all Indian languages. He noted that while Maharashtra is his professional base, every language in the country deserves appreciation and respect.

title: Kiran Joneja Defends Husband Ramesh Sippy After Interviewer Labels Him a ‘Colourful Man’ with Alleged Affairs.
content: Sippy, following remarks made during an interview where he was described as a "colourful man" with a past involving multiple affairs. Speaking to Lehren Retro, Kiran emphasized that commitment, rather than marriage itself, holds paramount importance in her life. She stated that throughout their three decades of marriage, she has never heard any rumours or reports regarding Ramesh’s alleged affairs. When pressed about the claims of past relationships, Kiran responded calmly, acknowledging that if such incidents occurred, they belong to the past and she prefers not to dwell on them. Kiran, who married Ramesh Sippy in 1991 when she was 27 and he was 44, highlighted her firm belief in their relationship despite the interviewer’s repeated insistence about Ramesh’s previous involvements with actresses. She maintained that after meeting her, Ramesh has not been involved in any affairs to her knowledge. Ramesh Sippy, previously married, is a father to three children Rohan, Sonya, and Sheena Sippy. Kiran Joneja is a seasoned actress known for her roles in films like Mulzim, Ek Misaal, Khosla Ka Ghosla, Jab We Met, Fashion, and Shimla Mirchi. She has also made her mark in television with popular serials such as Yeh Jo Hai Zindagi, Buniyaad, Mahabharat, and Junoon. Meanwhile, Ramesh Sippy’s illustrious directorial career includes iconic films like Sholay (1975), Seeta Aur Geeta (1972), Shaam (1980), and Saagar (1985), along with television works like Buniyaad (1986) and Gaatha (1997).

title: ICC Criticized for Controversial Verdict on Mohammed Siraj Amid Shubman Gill's On-Air Language Incident.
content: The International Cricket Council (ICC) faced criticism after docking 15 percent of Mohammed Siraj’s match fees for his fiery send-off to Ben Duckett during the Lord’s Test. Former England pacer Stuart Broad openly questioned the ICC’s inconsistency in enforcing the Code of Conduct, highlighting that while Siraj was penalized for his actions on Day 4, India captain Shubman Gill was seemingly let off despite swearing on live television during the same match. During the final session of Day 3, tensions escalated when Shubman Gill charged towards the stumps and told England’s Zak Crawley to “grow some b**” after time-wasting tactics. The aggressive atmosphere carried into Day 4, where Siraj confronted Duckett after taking his wicket, leading to a heated exchange that included close physical contact. These incidents have sparked debate about the ICC’s approach to disciplinary measures in high-profile matches. Broad emphasized the need for consistent enforcement of the ICC Code of Conduct, arguing that either both players should have been fined or neither punished. He stated, “Consistency is key,” underscoring that fair and uniform application of rules is essential to maintain respect and discipline in international cricket. This controversy has reignited discussions on how the ICC manages player behavior and penalties during major tournaments.

title: Ravi Shastri Advises Shubman Gill and Gautam Gambhir to 'Let Rishabh Pant Play,' but India Captain Takes a Different Path Moments Later.
content: Ahead of Day 5 of the third Test against England at Lord’s, former India head coach Ravi Shastri gave a strong piece of advice to the current Indian management. He urged skipper Shubman Gill and head coach Gautam Gambhir to avoid any discussions with Rishabh Pant before the start of play. Speaking on Sky Sports, Shastri emphasized that any attempt to talk strategy or give instructions might only confuse Pant and disrupt his natural game. Shastri highlighted that Pant is an experienced campaigner who fully understands the magnitude of the situation. With a Test average close to 45 and centuries in challenging overseas conditions, Pant doesn't need to be reminded of his responsibilities. According to Shastri, Pant knows what is expected from him and how crucial his innings is if India wants to secure victory. Importantly, Shastri stressed that Pant must play the way he knows best. Trying to mold him into a different kind of player or burdening him with instructions could backfire. “His computer is working,” Shastri said, implying that Pant's instincts and cricketing intelligence should be trusted. Letting him play freely and instinctively is the best approach for India on the final day.

title: Shubhanshu Shukla and Crew Return to Earth After 18-Day Space Mission as Axiom-4 Spacecraft Touches Down Safely.
content: The SpaceX Dragon spacecraft, carrying Indian astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla and three other Axiom-4 mission crew members, completed a successful splashdown off the coast of California at 3 pm IST on Tuesday, July 15. The spacecraft undocked from the International Space Station (ISS) around 4:50 pm IST on Monday and took approximately 22 and a half hours to return to Earth after an 18-day mission during which the crew conducted over 60 experiments. Piloted by Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO), the Ax-4 spacecraft, named ‘Grace,’ landed safely in the Pacific Ocean before the crew was retrieved by helicopters for a re-acclimatisation period. This mission follows a precedent set by Indian-origin astronaut Sunita Williams, who landed in a SpaceX capsule called ‘Freedom’ after a nine-month ISS mission earlier this year. Axiom-4 is the 18th crewed spaceflight operated by SpaceX since 2020 and highlights Axiom’s growing role in commercial spaceflight, providing access to low-Earth orbit for private and international astronauts. Additionally, Axiom is developing a commercial space station intended to succeed the ISS, which NASA plans to retire around 2030. The crew included Commander Peggy Whitson, a former NASA astronaut and Axiom employee, alongside international astronauts Shubhanshu Shukla from India, Slawosz Uznanski-Wisniewski from Poland, and Tibor Kapu from Hungary. For these countries, this mission marks a significant return to crewed spaceflight after decades. For India, it represents progress toward its first independent crewed mission under the Gaganyaan program, scheduled for 2027. During their 18-day stay aboard the ISS, the crew conducted experiments focused on medicine, agriculture, and space exploration, investigating the effects of microgravity on food production, water management, and human physiology.

title: America’s Largest Rare-Earth Producer Aims to Challenge China’s Market Dominance.
content: MP Materials is ambitiously positioning itself as a national leader in rare-earth production, aiming to directly challenge China’s longstanding dominance in the sector. This goal has gained significant momentum, fueled in part by strategic investments from the Pentagon, reflecting the critical importance of rare-earth metals for national security and advanced technology. At an industrial facility in Fort Worth, Texas, workers clad in full protective gear handle molten metal heated to 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit, producing rare-earth metals on a commercial scale—a feat the U.S. has seldom achieved in recent decades. This factory stands as a testament to MP Materials’ bold, billion-dollar commitment to reviving American capacity in a market largely controlled by China. The company has poured over $1 billion into upgrading infrastructure and equipment, while also managing a key mine in California. These efforts underscore MP Materials’ dedication to establishing a domestic supply chain for these vital metals, which are essential components in everything from electric vehicles and smartphones to advanced missile systems.

title: Washington Sundar’s cockiness fired up England; Indian star roasted for ‘horrible’ claim: ‘McCullum told everyone.
content: Washington Sundar’s confident prediction ahead of Day 5 at Lord’s unexpectedly fired up the England team. In a bold interview, Sundar claimed India would win the match within the first session, a statement that backfired as India ultimately collapsed and lost by 22 runs, handing England a 2-1 lead in the five-match series. The match was intense and closely contested, with India appearing in control until a sudden top-order collapse in their 193-run chase. Jos Buttler reflected on Sundar’s remark, suggesting it played a crucial role in motivating the English side. He highlighted how such statements don’t go unnoticed in the modern game and noted a rare moment when England’s head coach Brendon McCullum rallied the team, urging them to “ramp it up” in response to Sundar’s boldness. Buttler described the impact as a rallying cry that energized the dressing room ahead of the decisive day. Sundar’s innings, which began at a critical juncture alongside Ravindra Jadeja, ended quickly as he was dismissed for a four-ball duck by Jofra Archer. Despite Jadeja’s resilient 61* off 181 balls, and tail-enders Jasprit Bumrah and Mohammed Siraj providing some resistance, India couldn’t recover from the collapse. The lack of substantial support from the lower order ultimately sealed their fate in a thrilling and hard-fought Test.

title: Arijit Singh Makes Directorial Debut with Unique Jungle Adventure; Pan-India Project Produced by Mahaveer Jain.
content: Arijit Singh, widely celebrated as a legendary singer, is now set to make his debut as a film director in Bollywood. According to sources, he will helm a unique jungle adventure film produced by Mahaveer Jain, who is enthusiastic about creating a project that resonates with audiences across India. The film is currently in the casting and preparation stages, with the official announcement planned once the title and cast are finalized. This venture marks a significant expansion for both Arijit Singh and Mahaveer Jain Films. Alongside this project, Mahaveer Jain is producing several other notable films, including "NaagZilla" starring Kartik Aaryan and the international thriller "White," inspired by the life of spiritual leader Sri Sri Ravi Shankar, featuring Vikrant Massey. These projects contribute to an exciting slate under Jain’s production banner, demonstrating a diverse and ambitious approach to filmmaking. Arijit Singh’s directorial debut is co-written by him and Koyel Singh, with production led by Sudipta Bhattacharya, Mrighdeep Singh Lamba, and Bala Saheb Darade under Mahaveer Jain Films and Alokdyuti Films, with co-production from God Bless Entertainment. This film promises to showcase Singh’s creative talents beyond music, marking a new chapter in his artistic journey.

title: Sanjay Leela Bhansali to Film Epic Showdown Between Ranbir Kapoor and Vicky Kaushal for Love & War.
content: Sanjay Leela Bhansali's upcoming film Love & War, starring Ranbir Kapoor, Vicky Kaushal, and Alia Bhatt, is generating immense excitement among moviegoers. As anticipation continues to grow, a thrilling update has surfaced about a major showdown between Ranbir Kapoor and Vicky Kaushal’s characters, promising a cinematic spectacle like never before. An industry insider revealed that the film will feature a monumental face-off between the two leading actors, describing the sequence as potentially one of the grandest ever filmed in Indian cinema. Preparations for this epic scene are already underway, with the shoot planned at a lavish, yet undisclosed location. Early reports suggest the moment will be crafted as a major cinematic event designed to captivate audiences. With two of Bollywood’s finest talents going head-to-head under Bhansali’s direction, Love & War is shaping up to be a unique and unforgettable experience. Fans eagerly await the release of this powerhouse collaboration, scheduled for March 20, 2026, promising a blend of intense drama and masterful storytelling from the celebrated filmmaker and his stellar cast.

title: Vidyut Jammwal Set to Debut in Hollywood as Dhalsim in Live-Action Street Fighter Movie.
content: Actor Vidyut Jammwal is set to make his Hollywood debut as Dhalsim in the highly anticipated live-action adaptation of the iconic Street Fighter video game franchise. Produced by Legendary Entertainment, the film brings to life the beloved characters from Capcom’s classic fighting game series. Directed by Kitao Sakurai, the project boasts an impressive ensemble cast featuring Andrew Koji as Ryu, Noah Centineo as Ken, Callina Liang as Chun-Li, Jason Momoa as Blanka, and Jammwal as the powerful yogi Dhalsim. Known for his exceptional martial arts skills and stunt work, Vidyut Jammwal’s casting as Dhalsim perfectly blends his action star persona with the character’s unique spiritual strength and fiery abilities. This role marks a significant milestone in Jammwal’s career, expanding his global presence and showcasing his talents in an international action film. Fans of the Street Fighter series eagerly await the film, which promises to stay true to the original game’s spirit centered around the global fighting tournament led by the villain M. Bison, played by David Dastmalchian. The upcoming Street Fighter movie builds on the franchise’s legacy as one of the most successful fighting game series worldwide, having sold over 55 million copies since its launch in 1987. Following previous adaptations like the 1994 film starring Jean-Claude Van Damme and the 2009 reboot focusing on Chun-Li, this new film aims to capture the excitement and energy of the beloved game while introducing a fresh, star-studded cast to fans across the globe.

title: Udaipur Files row: After Delhi HC stay, petition filed with I&B Ministry to ban Vijay Raaz starrer.
content: Jamiat Ulama-i-Hind, one of the petitioners, has formally approached the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting (I&B) seeking a ban on its release. Originally slated for a July 11, 2025 release, the film’s debut was halted following a stay order from the Delhi High Court, which directed the Centre to reassess the certification granted by the Central Board of Film Certification (CBFC). Arshad Madani, president of Jamiat Ulama-i-Hind, confirmed through his official X account that the petition was filed in compliance with the court’s directive. Madani emphasized that the petition raises concerns that Udaipur Files could incite communal tension and harm India’s international image. The Ministry has been asked to respond within a week, with a hearing anticipated shortly. The film reportedly depicts the murder of Kanhaiya Lal Teli, a tailor from Udaipur, who was killed in June 2022 after allegedly supporting controversial remarks by BJP spokesperson Nupur Sharma about Prophet Muhammad. This incident sparked widespread protests and national outrage. Petitioners argue that the film risks distorting communal narratives and promoting hate, despite the filmmakers' claims of presenting an accurate portrayal of events. The Delhi High Court has urged the Centre to carefully reconsider the CBFC’s certification in light of all objections before deciding on the film’s release, highlighting the sensitivity of the subject matter and its potential social impact.

title: Shweta Tripathi to Produce Debut Film Mujhe Jaan Na Kaho Meri Jaan Featuring Herself and Tillotama Shome.
content: Earlier this year, actress Shweta Tripathi announced her debut as a producer with a queer drama titled Mujhe Jaan Na Kaho Meri Jaan. The film will feature Shweta herself alongside acclaimed actress Tillotama Shome, who will play a pivotal role. Directed by Sanjoy Nag, this queer love story aims to challenge stereotypes and ignite meaningful conversations. The project is scheduled to begin filming by the end of 2025. This marks the first collaboration between Shweta Tripathi and Tillotama Shome, adding emotional depth and resonance to the film. The venture follows the success of Shweta’s previous queer-themed production, the play Cock, which was praised for its honest portrayal of identity and love. Encouraged by the positive reception, Shweta is now expanding her vision into cinema with this significant project. Expressing her enthusiasm, Shweta shared that the film is close to her heart not only as her first production but also for its representation of queer love stories told with honesty and nuance. She expressed admiration and trust for Tillotama, highlighting their long-standing desire to work together. With this film, Shweta aims to bring underrepresented voices to the forefront and create a narrative that is both tender and transformative, with more details on the cast and release dates to be announced soon.

title: Mamata Banerjee leads a large-scale protest in Kolkata against the "harassment" of Bengali-speaking people.
content: Senior Trinamool Congress leaders joined Mamata Banerjee in a protest in Kolkata against the harassment of Bengali speakers in states ruled by the BJP. Mamata Banerjee, the chief minister of West Bengal, led a demonstration in Kolkata on Wednesday afternoon in protest of the alleged harassment of Bengali speakers in states ruled by the Bharatiya Janata Party. Along with thousands of people, Mamata Banerjee took to the streets, accompanied by senior party leaders, such as Abhishek Banerjee, national general secretary of the Trinamool Congress. According to news agency PTI, the march will end at Dorina Crossing in Dharmatala after starting at College Square in central Kolkata at approximately 1:45 p.m.There were almost 1,500 police officers on duty to guard the barricaded pavements and nearby buildings along the nearly 3-kilometer route. Traffic was rerouted along several

title: Sidhu Moose Wala's Posthumous World Tour Announced: Fans React With Surprise and Curiosity
content: Three years after his tragic death, legendary Punjabi singer-rapper Sidhu Moose Wala is set to make a global return virtually. On Tuesday, his official Instagram account stunned fans by announcing the “Signed to God World Tour” scheduled for 2026. A teaser video posted online showed Sidhu performing on stage, sparking curiosity and excitement. The caption was minimal, simply reading “2026,” without any details about the format, performers, or cities involved. While Sidhu’s posthumous music releases have kept his legacy alive, this announcement hints at an even grander posthumous presence. The tour is being organized by Platinum Events and promoted through a dedicated website featuring a heartfelt tribute to Sidhu’s cultural influence. Describing him as more than a musician a symbol of youth and a global movement—the site quotes his famous line: “Mere baare likheya jaavega, history de vich” (They’ll write about me in history). Fans were left both thrilled and puzzled, with speculation rising about whether this could be a hologram tour using Sidhu’s pre-recorded performances, similar to late rap legend Tupac Shakur’s virtual stage appearances. Key Reactions and Takeaways: Instagram video teaser garnered over a million likes in 24 hours. Fans flooded the comments with "Welcome back" and heartfelt messages. Many questioned, “Who’s going to perform?” while others joked about the surreal announcement. Speculations suggest a hologram performance tour, combining tech and unreleased content. The mystery behind the format and execution has only deepened the buzz around Sidhu’s legacy. Ask ChatGPT

title: Salman Khan's 'Battle of Galwan' Skips Eid Release, Likely to Hit Theatres in Early or Mid 2026.
content: After creating massive buzz with Sikandar (2025), Bollywood megastar Salman Khan is now ready to take his fans on another thrilling ride with his upcoming war drama, Battle Of Galwan. The film’s first look has already captivated audiences, showing Salman in a never-seen-before, intense military avatar. But while fans expected a traditional Eid release something that has long been a Salman signature there’s now confirmation that Battle Of Galwan won’t hit theatres on Eid 2026. According to a trusted source from Bollywood Hungama, the makers have deliberately avoided the Eid release window due to intense box office competition. As of now, three major films are scheduled for March 19, 2026 Yash’s Toxic, Dhamaal 4, and Sanjay Leela Bhansali’s Love & War, starring Ranbir Kapoor, Alia Bhatt, and Vicky Kaushal. Although there's speculation that Love & War may shift its date, Salman Khan and his team are known for playing fair and avoiding unnecessary release clashes. “Currently, the team is considering two possible release windows for Battle Of Galwan January 2026 or June 2026,” the source revealed. “Ramzan begins in late February, so that rules out a March-April release. Also, Bakri Eid on May 27 is too close to the IPL finale on May 31, which could affect footfalls.” Another insider added, “The shoot is expected to wrap up in around 55-60 days, which means January is still a viable option. If post-production takes longer, then June 2026 will likely be finalized.” Despite the absence of a festive release date, expectations are sky-high. Salman Khan’s star power has rarely relied on festivals alone films like Wanted, Tiger Zinda Hai, and Sultan have shown that his fan base turns out in huge numbers, holiday or not.

title: Zomato CEO Deepinder Goyal Clears the Air: “No, I Haven’t Bought a Private Jet  But Yeah, It’d Be Nice Someday.
content: Zomato CEO clarifies aviation startup LAT hasn’t bought a jet; hints at future possibility with a wink Zomato CEO Deepinder Goyal has denied recent reports claiming he purchased a private jet through a startup he backs. The clarification comes after a Moneycontrol report suggested that LAT Aerospace, an aviation startup linked to Goyal, had acquired a Bombardier Global jet, now parked at Delhi airport. Taking to X (formerly Twitter), Goyal called the report “clickbaity” and stated, “LAT is a startup focused on building planes for regional aviation – not buying them.” While dismissing the current ownership, Goyal playfully responded “One day” to a comment suggesting he should own a Gulfstream G650, sparking speculation about future ambitions. The timing of the report raised eyebrows as it followed Goyal’s recent purchase of a ₹52.3 crore luxury apartment in Gurugram’s DLF Camellias. While Goyal maintains he does not currently own a jet, his brief response has kept fans and followers curious. With a net worth of $1.6 billion, according to Forbes, the idea of private luxury isn’t far-fetched but for now, Goyal’s feet remain firmly on the ground.

title: After Superman, Now Saiyaara Feels the Heat: CBFC Cuts 10 Seconds of Intimate Scenes for “Sensuality.
content: As someone who's been in the film and entertainment business for a while, I can tell you this — whenever a Mohit Suri film drops, expectations run high. Saiyaara, starring fresh faces Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda, is no exception. Slated to release this Friday, July 18, the film has already caught serious traction thanks to a catchy soundtrack, a sizzling trailer, and the YRF name backing it. The censor process is done and dusted, but not without the usual tweaks. The CBFC has passed it with a U/A 16+ certificate, but asked for a few changes. Four “objectionable” words have been toned down, though the actual list isn’t out. More notably, they cut 10 seconds of intimate visuals, calling them “too sensual” or exposing too much. They also added a helmet safety disclaimer in bike scenes which, to be fair, is a smart call in today’s context. After last week’s Superman edit drama, it’s not surprising that Saiyaara too saw a bit of censorship. Still, that hasn't slowed the hype. Here’s the exciting bit Saiyaara is set for a solid opening. As of Wednesday morning, it's already sold 45,000+ tickets across major chains: 33,500 in PVR Inox, 11,500 in Cinepolis, and 2,059 in MovieMax. That’s a strong indicator of audience interest. If the word of mouth kicks in post-Friday, we could be looking at a double-digit opening and that’s no small feat for a romantic drama.

title: From Bajrangi Bhaijaan to Jab We Met: Heartwarming Bollywood Tearjerkers Where Strangers Become Family.
content: As Salman Khan’s Bajrangi Bhaijaan celebrates 10 years since its release, it remains a powerful example of Bollywood’s ability to portray strangers coming together as family. The film tells the story of Pawan, a devoted man who protects a lost mute girl from Pakistan and helps reunite her with her family, creating an emotional bond that transcends borders. This heartfelt connection, especially when Munni calls Pawan “Mama,” has left a lasting impact on audiences. Bollywood offers many such touching stories where strangers form deep bonds. In Vivah, Shahid Kapoor and Amrita Rao’s characters start as strangers in an arranged marriage but gradually build a loving family through support and understanding. Similarly, Jab We Met follows the journey of lively Geet and reserved Aditya, whose unexpected companionship grows into a meaningful relationship that feels like family by the end. Other films like Bareilly Ki Barfi and Jab Harry Met Sejal also explore this theme beautifully. Whether it’s Bitti’s search for her perfect match or Harry and Sejal’s journey of self-discovery and connection, these stories highlight how shared experiences and kindness can transform strangers into family, creating some of Bollywood’s most emotional moments.

title: IndiGo Flight from Delhi to Imphal Turns Back Mid-Air Due to Technical Issue.
content: An IndiGo flight from Delhi to Imphal was forced to return to Delhi shortly after takeoff on Thursday due to a minor technical issue. The pilots detected the snag soon after the flight departed and, following safety protocols, decided to turn back as a precaution. The aircraft landed safely at Indira Gandhi International Airport without any complications. After landing, the plane underwent thorough checks in accordance with mandatory procedures. Once the necessary inspections were completed and the issue was resolved, the flight resumed its journey to Imphal. IndiGo confirmed that the safety and well-being of passengers remained their top priority throughout the incident. This event comes just days after another IndiGo flight from Delhi to Goa had to make an emergency landing in Mumbai due to an engine issue mid-air. In both situations, the airline responded quickly and efficiently to ensure passenger safety, with no reported injuries in either case.

title: Kiara Advani’s Heartfelt Wish for Her Daughter to Grow Up with This Superstar’s Confidence and Charm.
content: Bollywood actors Sidharth Malhotra and Kiara Advani have entered a new phase in their lives as proud parents. The couple welcomed their first child, a baby girl, on July 15 in Mumbai, bringing immense joy to their family and fans alike. This milestone has been warmly received across the film industry and beyond. The news of their daughter’s birth quickly spread, sparking an outpouring of love and heartfelt congratulations from supporters, friends, and fellow celebrities. Social media platforms have been flooded with warm wishes celebrating the arrival of the little bundle of joy. Fans are excited to see this new chapter unfold for the beloved couple. Sidharth and Kiara’s journey into parenthood marks a significant moment both personally and professionally. As they embrace this joyful role, well-wishers are eager to follow their journey as a family. This happy occasion adds another beautiful story to their lives, inspiring many with their love and commitment.

title: Vidya Malavade Remembers Her First Husband 25 Years After His Tragic Air India Plane Crash: “That Loss Hurt.
content: Actor Vidya Malavade recently shared a heartfelt message on Instagram, remembering her late first husband, Captain Arvind Singh Bagga, who passed away 25 years ago. In her emotional post, Vidya expressed how deeply she still misses him his presence, laughter, and the feeling of safety he gave her. She reflected on the lasting impact of his loss and how it has taught her to cherish every moment with loved ones, acknowledging life’s fragility and unpredictability. Vidya also opened up about the recent tragic Air India plane crash on June 12 near Ahmedabad, which claimed 242 lives, including the memory of her late husband’s own crash as a pilot in 2000. The incident brought back vivid memories and emotions for her, reminding her of the pain and strength she’s carried over the years. She emphasized that living with gratitude and surrender has helped her find peace despite the grief, viewing Arvind as her guardian angel and guiding light. Closing her post, Vidya quoted lyrics from Celine Dion’s “My Heart Will Go On,” a song that held special meaning for them. Her touching tribute resonates with many, highlighting the enduring power of love and remembrance even after loss, while reminding us all to value those closest to us every day

title: Flipkart GOAT Sale 2025: Great Deals on Top Split and Window ACs with Discounts Up to 51%.
content: Stay cool this summer with the Flipkart Sale 2025, offering great discounts on a wide range of air conditioners. Whether you’re looking for powerful and energy-efficient Split ACs or budget-friendly Window ACs, top brands like Voltas, LG, Daikin, Blue Star, Hitachi, and Samsung have you covered. These ACs come with features like inverter technology, fast cooling, and smart connectivity, perfect for keeping your home comfortable while saving on electricity bills. Split ACs are ideal for larger rooms and living spaces, providing silent operation and consistent cooling, while Window ACs are compact and easy to install, suited for smaller rooms. With capacities ranging from 1 ton to 2 tons, you can choose the right model based on your room size and cooling needs. Don’t miss out on these limited-time deals from trusted brands known for durability and advanced technology. title: Indian Businessman Sells Stake in ₹6,800 Crore Company, Cites Lack of Interest from Younger Generation to Take Over.
content: Dilip Piramal, Chairman of VIP Industries, announced that the younger generation in his family is not interested in managing the business. Amid declining market share and recent losses, the company decided to sell a 32% stake to private equity investors. VIP Industries, valued at ₹6,830 crore, has seen fluctuating stock prices over the past year. Piramal expressed regret for not selling earlier when shares were valued higher. He recently sold the 32% stake for ₹1,763 crore, marking the end of his 52-year leadership at VIP Industries.

title: Karun Nair’s Comeback in Doubt as India Opts for Sai Sudharsan Ahead of England Tour.
content: ndia are likely to make one change for the crucial fourth Test against England starting July 23 at Old Trafford, with Karun Nair expected to be replaced by young Sai Sudharsan. Nair, who returned to the Test side after eight years, has failed to make a strong impact, getting starts but not converting them into big scores. His struggles at number three, particularly against deliveries that bounce from a length, have concerned the team management. His dismissal in the second innings at Lord’s, where he left an incoming ball from Brydon Carse, highlighted issues with his judgment. With India trailing 1-2 in the series and needing a win to stay alive, the selectors are leaning towards the 23-year-old Sudharsan. Despite a decent debut, he was earlier dropped to adjust the team combination. Now, with pressure mounting, the management may look to back fresh talent.

title: Renuka Shahane Shares Thoughts on Marathi-Hindi Language Controversy in Maharashtra: Says Violence Isn’t the Answer.
content: Veteran actress Renuka Shahane has spoken out about the ongoing tension between Marathi and Hindi speakers in Maharashtra, urging people to focus on respectful dialogue rather than resorting to aggression. In light of recent incidents where people were allegedly slapped for not speaking Marathi, Shahane stressed that such behavior does more harm than good to the cause of preserving any language . She pointed out that languages grow through love, culture, and daily use—not by enforcing them through intimidation. According to her, using violence to promote Marathi will only create fear and division, and it risks turning people away from the language rather than encouraging them to learn and embrace it. Shahane emphasized that promoting Marathi should be a matter of pride, not pressure. Calling for mutual respect and understanding, Shahane said Maharashtra has always been known for its inclusive and cultured society. Instead of creating language-based divides, she urged citizens to celebrate the richness of all Indian languages. In her view, meaningful efforts like including Marathi in educational systems, media, and everyday communication are more impactful ways to strengthen the language without alienating others.

title: "Bomb Threats Spark Panic as Over 80 Schools in Delhi and Bengaluru Face Security Searches.
content: Nearly 100 schools across India were hit by bomb threats this week, causing widespread panic among students, parents, and school authorities. On Friday alone, around 50 schools in Delhi received alarming emails claiming explosives were planted on campus. Well-known institutions like St. Xavier’s in Civil Lines, Richmond Global School in Paschim Vihar, and The Sovereign School in Rohini were among those targeted. This marks the fourth consecutive day that schools in the national capital have received such threats. In a parallel incident, about 40 private schools in Bengaluru, especially in areas like Rajarajeshwari Nagar and Kengeri, also received similar emails with chilling subject lines like “Bombs inside the school.” The threatening messages, sent from an anonymous email address, included graphic descriptions and claimed TNT had been hidden in school premises. Following these alerts, police and bomb disposal teams were deployed, and large-scale search operations were launched across affected schools. So far, no explosives have been found, and officials believe the threats are hoaxes. However, investigations are ongoing. Most of the emails were traced to encrypted networks and VPNs, making it extremely difficult to identify the source. A Delhi Police official compared the chase to “tracking a shadow in a room full of mirrors.”

title: Watch Akshay Kumar’s Hit Movies Online as Housefull 5 Arrives on OTT.
content: The fifth installment of the popular Housefull franchise, Housefull 5, is now available to rent on Prime Video. Both versions of the film Housefull 5A and Housefull 5B have dropped on the platform, but will be free to watch for all subscribers starting August 1. Starring Akshay Kumar, Riteish Deshmukh, Chunky Pandey, Abhishek Bachchan, Sonam Bajwa, Nargis Fakhri, Sanjay Dutt, Jackie Shroff, and Nana Patekar, the film had a mixed response at the box office. Now, audiences have a chance to catch it online and see if it lives up to the franchise’s reputation. While you wait for Housefull 5 to become free to stream, you can enjoy Akshay Kumar’s other hit films on OTTplay Premium a great way to revisit some of his most entertaining performances.

title: Manisha Koirala Beams with Joy After Receiving Honorary Doctorate in the UK: ‘Truly Grateful for This Honour.
content: Manisha Koirala has been awarded an Honorary Doctorate by the University of Bradford in the UK, and she couldn’t be happier. The actor took to Instagram to share her joy, posting a picture from the ceremony along with a heartfelt note. She said receiving this recognition, especially during the year Bradford was named UK City of Culture 2025, was a great honour. She also expressed her delight in sharing the moment with Burberry's Creative Director, Daniel Lee. In a video posted earlier, Manisha spoke about how she didn’t follow the traditional educational path, but instead learned through life experiences through hard work, setbacks, and resilience. She said the honour means more than she can express and serves as proof that everyone’s journey matters, regardless of how it begins. Manisha started her career with the Nepali film Pheri Bhetaula and made her Bollywood debut with Saudagar in 1991. She went on to star in iconic films like Bombay, 1942: A Love Story, Dil Se, and Company. After a break, she returned with Lust Stories in 2018 and recently played a powerful role in Sanjay Leela Bhansali’s Heeramandi, which has been renewed for a second season.

title: Why Was Former Chhattisgarh CM Bhupesh Baghel's Son Chaitanya Taken Into ED Custody.
content: Chaitanya Baghel, son of former Chhattisgarh Chief Minister Bhupesh Baghel, was arrested by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) on Friday in connection with a liquor scam. The arrest took place at his Bhilai residence after an early morning raid by ED officials. He was taken into custody under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) and later sent to five days of ED custody. This arrest is part of a broader investigation into alleged irregularities in the state's liquor policy during the Congress government’s rule. The ED’s case is based on an FIR filed by the Chhattisgarh Anti-Corruption Bureau, which named around 70 individuals and entities. The agency suspects that nearly ₹1,000 crore worth of proceeds from the scam were laundered through real estate firms linked to Chaitanya and his associates. In response, the Congress party walked out of the Chhattisgarh Assembly in protest. Leaders accused the ED of targeting Opposition members and their families. Bhupesh Baghel claimed the arrest was politically motivated, aiming to distract from the Adani issue that was set to be raised in the Assembly. He alleged the Centre was misusing agencies to suppress dissent.

title: Take a Look Inside Sonu Sood’s 'Om Shanti Om'-Style Bungalow Featuring Fan Art and a Special Shoe Room.
content: Filmmaker Farah Khan recently visited Sonu Sood’s stunning Mumbai bungalow and gave viewers a warm, engaging tour through her vlog. From the moment you step inside, the house gives off a mix of elegance and personal charm. Inspired by the film Om Shanti Om, the interiors feature soothing tones, stylish furniture, and plenty of thoughtful touches. A large Buddha statue greets visitors at the entrance, and the spacious living room is lit beautifully, complete with a beige and white sofa set and a grand staircase. The dining area nearby adds a homely, welcoming vibe. Farah explored the bedrooms of Sonu’s sons, Ishant and Ayaan, each designed with unique character. Ishant’s classy room features a cool grey theme and a large window, which he joked had once been his escape route. Ayaan’s room stands out with wooden flooring, a charming balcony surrounded by greenery, and an eye-catching anime painting gifted by a fan he had helped. Sonu also showed off a cozy media room where he hosts film meetings and proudly displayed a thread painting gifted by another fan who came all the way from his hometown. One of the standout features of the home is a dedicated shoe room a dream for any sneaker lover. Sonu, who shares his collection with his sons, has neatly organized his sneakers in a transparent wardrobe. The home also includes a modern modular kitchen and a small temple space, keeping a balance between style and spirituality. During the vlog, Sonu prepared avocado toast and healthy wraps for Farah and Dilip, as they chatted about old times filming Happy New Year. Fans praised the home for its warmth, simplicity, and the way it honors fan contributions with heartfelt artwork.

title: Chunky Panday Sends Love to Nephew Ahaan on Saiyaara Release Day
content: As Saiyaara, starring Ahaan Panday, hits theatres today, his uncle and veteran actor Chunky Panday shared a heartfelt message to mark the special occasion. Taking to Instagram, Chunky posted a series of nostalgic childhood photos of Ahaan alongside his daughter Ananya Panday. Along with the pictures, he wished his nephew the very best for his big debut, writing, “My Saiyaara ahaanpandayy, may you shine the brightest in our galaxy full of stars. Wishing you all the love and luck for today and forever.” Ananya Panday also welcomed her cousin into the film industry with an emotional post. Sharing her excitement, she said she’s been obsessed with Ahaan since the very beginning and now can’t wait for the world to feel the same. “Can’t believe my little bean’s first movie comes out tomorrow. Welcome to the movies, Ahaaani! The sweetest boy,” she wrote, adding the hashtag #Saiyaara. Directed by Mohit Suri, known for films like Zeher, Kalyug, Woh Lamhe, Awarapan, and Aashiqui 2, Saiyaara marks his first collaboration with Yash Raj Films. Calling it a tribute to the romantic films that shaped him, Suri said Saiyaara is a celebration of the timeless love stories and emotional journeys that have inspired him throughout his life. Ask ChatGPT

title: What Is Cloud Seeding? Delhi Set to Start Trials in September to Fight Air Pollution.
content: Delhi is preparing to conduct its first cloud seeding trial in early September to tackle rising air pollution by inducing artificial rain. This method involves spraying substances like silver iodide or sodium chloride into clouds to encourage rainfall, which can help wash pollutants out of the air. The project, led by IIT-Kanpur, was originally planned for July but was delayed based on weather inputs from IMD and other agencies. The new timeline coincides with the retreating monsoon, offering better cloud conditions. The Delhi government has approved ₹3.21 crore for the pilot, which will be executed by IIT-Kanpur’s Aerospace Engineering Department. The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has cleared all flight operations. A specially equipped Cessna 206-H aircraft will carry out five test flights over pollution-prone areas like Rohini, Bawana, and parts of Uttar Pradesh including Loni and Baghpat. Delhi Environment Minister Manjinder Singh Sirsa confirmed that all safety protocols are in place, and the aircraft will avoid restricted air zones. He said the experiment aims to improve air quality during the post-monsoon period when pollution levels typically rise. If successful, the initiative could offer a new way to manage Delhi’s air pollution crisis.

title: Sanjay Gupta Criticizes Gimmicky Film Promotions, Praises Saiyaara’s Subtle Approach.
content: Saiyaara, starring newcomers Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda, hit theatres this Friday with minimal pre-release promotions no interviews, appearances, or podcasts. Despite that, the film is off to a strong start, with ₹9.39 crore in advance bookings and positive reviews, especially for its music and fresh performances. Filmmaker Sanjay Gupta praised the film’s marketing strategy, calling it a smart move by Yash Raj Films to preserve the excitement for the big screen. On social media, he criticized the current trend of excessive promotions pushed by corporate studios, saying it hurt the film industry more than helped. He pointed to Saiyaara and 12th Fail as examples of films succeeding without PR gimmicks, urging the industry to rethink its approach. Directed by Mohit Suri and produced by YRF CEO Akshaye Widhani, Saiyaara is gaining attention not just for its story, but also for its fresh release strategy. The film’s music, composed by Tanishk Bagchi, Arslan Nizami, and Faheem Abdullah, has also struck a chord with audiences.

title: Sitaare Zameen Par writer on Aamir Khan: Heard the 'interfering' claims, says he’s just deeply involved.
content: Writer Divy Nidhi Sharma, who has worked with Aamir Khan on Sitaare Zameen Par and Laapataa Ladies, has addressed the common perception that Aamir interferes in the creative process. In a recent chat with India Today, Divy clarified that although Aamir stays deeply involved in his projects, there’s no interference at all. “I had heard such things too,” Divy admitted, “but after working closely with him on Laapataa Ladies and now Sitaare Zameen Par, I can confidently say there was zero interference. I was present during the shoot, and even from director RS Prasanna’s side, it was one of the smoothest experiences we’ve had.” He went on to say that Aamir is someone who genuinely respects the written word. “He likes to be part of discussions and is open to debate. Yes, it takes him time to build trust, but once that’s there, the journey becomes very easy. Even if he had a different opinion, he would never impose it. He would ask the writer before making even a small change like a comma.” Sitaare Zameen Par, which released in theatres on June 20, has so far earned ₹164 crore in India. Directed by RS Prasanna, the film stars Aamir as a basketball coach mentoring ten differently-abled individuals. It also features Genelia D’Souza along with a cast of newcomers. The movie is seen as a spiritual sequel to the beloved Taare Zameen Par (2007).

title: PM Modi Criticizes RJD and TMC Over Scams and Infiltration Issues.
content: At a rally in Motihari, Bihar, Prime Minister Narendra Modi stepped up his criticism of the RJD, particularly over the "land-for-jobs" scam linked to Lalu Prasad Yadav and his family. He said only the NDA is capable of providing honest governance and real opportunities for people. Later, in West Bengal’s Durgapur, Modi took on the Trinamool Congress, accusing it of indulging in appeasement and vote-bank politics by supporting illegal infiltration from Bangladesh. He warned that such actions threaten both Bengal’s cultural identity and the country’s security. Modi also announced a major initiative worth ₹1 lakh crore aimed at helping young people land their first jobs. Starting August 1, those getting their first appointment in a private company will receive ₹15,000 from the central government. He also spoke about the Mudra scheme, which supports self-employment, noting that in just the past two months, lakhs of loans have been handed out in Bihar. The Prime Minister said that the problem of naxalism, which once affected districts like Champaran, Aurangabad, Gaya, and Jamui, has weakened significantly and will soon come to an end. He also highlighted the PM Dhan-Dhanya Krishi Yojana, which will identify 100 of the most backward agricultural districts and provide them with targeted support to uplift farmers and weaker communities.

title: Supreme Court says caste-based politics can harm the true essence of democracy
content: The Supreme Court has raised serious concerns about the rising trend of caste and regional politics, calling them just as harmful as communal politics. While hearing a petition against AIMIM’s registration, the bench, led by Justices Surya Kant and Joymalya Bagchi, remarked that seeking votes on the basis of caste or region poses a serious threat to the democratic spirit of the country. The judges stressed that this issue goes beyond one party and called for broader reforms in how political parties function. The case was brought forward by Shiv Sena member Tirupati Narasimha Murari, who argued that AIMIM’s constitution promotes religious identity and Islamic education, allegedly violating India’s secular values. However, the court found that the party’s stated aim is to work for the welfare of backward communities, which is allowed under the Constitution. The bench also clarified that reading or promoting religious literature, whether Islamic or otherwise, is not illegal, and that there was no strong evidence that AIMIM had violated any laws. While the court dismissed the plea to cancel AIMIM’s registration, it acknowledged the need for systemic political reforms. It encouraged raising these broader concerns through a more comprehensive petition, allowing the judiciary to consider inputs from all political parties. The petitioner then withdrew his case, with the freedom to file a new, expanded challenge focusing on caste, communal, and regional politics across the board.

title: Donald Trump Says Five Fighter Jets Were Downed During India-Pakistan Clash in May.
content: Former US President Donald Trump has once again stirred controversy by taking credit for helping defuse tensions between India and Pakistan, a claim India strongly disputes. During a dinner with Republican lawmakers at the White House, Trump said that “five jets were shot down” during the recent flare-up between the two nuclear-armed countries. However, he didn’t specify which country lost the aircraft, and there’s been no official confirmation to support his statement. Trump went on to say that his administration played a key role in averting a major conflict between India and Pakistan. He claimed that the US used trade talks as leverage, telling both sides that there would be no trade deal if they continued to escalate tensions. India, however, has consistently maintained that the ceasefire was the result of a direct conversation between the military leadership of both countries on May 10 and had nothing to do with outside pressure or mediation. India launched Operation Sindoor on May 7 in response to a terrorist attack in Pahalgam, targeting terrorist infrastructure in Pakistan-controlled areas. The operation led to four days of intense exchanges involving drones and missiles. According to Indian officials, the eventual ceasefire was agreed upon through direct communication between the Directors General of Military Operations (DGMOs) of both countries — not due to any international intervention.

title: Raj Thackeray’s Bold ‘Duba Duba Ke’ Remark to BJP MP Triggers Political Uproar in Maharashtra.
content: A heated war of words between Raj Thackeray and BJP MP Nishikant Dubey has sparked a political storm across Maharashtra. Their aggressive remarks with Thackeray threatening to “drown and thrash” in Mumbai’s sea and Dubey responding with threats to “beat up Marathi speakers” have drawn backlash, even from within the BJP-led alliance. The row escalated after the state government withdrew orders making Hindi a compulsory third language in schools, following opposition pressure. Shiv Sena’s Shaina NC slammed the language used by both leaders, calling it hooliganism. “What is this duba-duba, patak-patak? Speak of development, not threats,” she said, urging a shift towards constructive politics. BJP’s Sudhir Mungantiwar also condemned the threats, saying, “No one can drown someone in the sea; we live by the Constitution.” Meanwhile, Congress leader Surendra Rajput accused Dubey of stoking tensions in Mumbai for political mileage and criticized both him and Thackeray for divisive remarks. He also hit out at the Maharashtra government, claiming it was more interested in promoting Hindi than protecting Marathi. Raj Thackeray further warned that a larger agenda was at play, hinting that powerful business lobbies aimed to weaken Marathi influence in Mumbai.

title: 15-year-old girl set ablaze by three men in Odisha’s Puri, just days after Balasore student’s self-immolation incident.
content: A shocking incident took place in Bayabar village of Odisha’s Puri district where a 15-year-old girl was set on fire by three men while she was on her way to visit a friend. Eyewitnesses reported that the accused poured petrol on the girl and fled the scene. She was critically injured and rushed to AIIMS Bhubaneswar for treatment. The incident happened just days after a 20-year-old student from Fakir Mohan (Autonomous) College in Balasore set herself ablaze on campus, reportedly due to inaction on her sexual harassment complaint against a professor. The student succumbed to her injuries on July 14, and the professor involved was later arrested. The case has sparked statewide protests and outrage. Deputy Chief Minister Pravati Parida expressed deep shock over the Puri incident and confirmed that the state government will bear all medical expenses. He also directed the police to take immediate action and arrest the culprits. Meanwhile, a UGC fact-finding team visited Balasore to investigate the circumstances of the earlier case, as opposition parties continue to demand justice and accountability.

title: Sharda University student’s suicide triggers protests; police file FIR against five staff members.
content: A 20-year-old BDS student at Sharda University in Greater Noida was found dead in her hostel room on July 18, with police confirming it was a case of suicide by hanging. Her death sparked a major protest on campus, with her family and fellow students accusing university staff of mental harassment and negligence. According to the victim’s relatives, faculty members had falsely accused her of forging signatures, insulted her in class, and threatened to fail her, which they believe pushed her to take the extreme step. The student’s mother, Sunita, began a sit-in protest on the university campus, refusing to leave until justice was served. She emotionally appealed for help from top leaders, saying she would not move until action was taken. During the protest, students and the victim’s family claimed they were lathi-charged by the police, adding to the tension. Many BDS students reportedly felt unsafe and wanted to return home. Based on the family's complaint, an FIR was registered, naming five staff members. Two individuals were taken into custody for questioning. Police said they have asked the university to take preventive steps to avoid such tragedies in the future. A meeting between the administration and the victim’s family was arranged as the protest continued. Authorities stated the situation is now under control, and further investigation is ongoing.

title: Shah Rukh Khan injured during shoot for King, advised one month rest; travels to US for treatment.
content: Shah Rukh Khan recently got injured while filming an intense action scene for his upcoming movie King in Mumbai. The incident caused concern on set, but sources close to the production have said that the injury isn’t serious, though he has been advised to rest for a month. It’s believed to be a muscular injury, and Shah Rukh has flown to the US for medical treatment. Because of this, the film’s shooting schedule for July and August has been put on hold. The team now plans to resume filming sometime after September, depending on Shah Rukh's recovery. Until then, he’s been told to focus on healing and return to the set only when fully fit, with proper care. While there hasn’t been any official confirmation from his team, his manager Pooja Dadlani’s recent Instagram Story suggests she’s currently in New York, hinting at Shah Rukh’s presence there for treatment. Fans can rest assured that he’s recovering well and taking the necessary steps to get back to work soon.

title: Sponsors Back Out of WCL in Protest After India Legends Withdraw and Match Gets Cancelled: "We Support Our Team.
content: The India vs Pakistan match at the World Championship of Legends 2025, scheduled for July 20 in Edgbaston, has been officially cancelled following strong public backlash. The organisers faced intense criticism after announcing the game, especially in the wake of the Pahalgam terror attack that killed 26 tourists. As public sentiment turned against any sporting engagement with Pakistan, WCL issued a formal apology and confirmed that the match would not go ahead, acknowledging the hurt caused to the Indian public. EaseMyTrip, one of the main sponsors of the tournament, also released a firm statement reiterating its position. The company clarified that despite being in a five-year sponsorship deal with WCL, it had made it clear from the beginning that it would not associate with or support any match involving Pakistan. “We proudly support Team India, but we will not engage in any game that includes Pakistan. Bharat First. Always,” the company said. The WCL organisers explained that the match was initially planned after it was announced that Pakistan's hockey team would be touring India. However, with rising tensions and a flood of social media appeals urging Indian players not to compete against Pakistan, the decision was reversed. The organisers have now promised to be more mindful of public sentiment in the future.

title: Sanjay Dutt Shares Why He Chose The Bhootnii: "I Was Hooked From the Very First Page.
content: Talking about the role, Dutt said he didn’t approach it any differently. “As actors, our job is to make every role believable. Whether it’s a gangster or a ghostbuster, the effort is the same,” he shared. He also spoke about the charm of horror-comedies, saying they’re fun because they manage to scare and entertain at the same time. According to him, The Bhootnii brings back the nostalgic feel of classic horror-comedies while keeping things modern and engaging. What really convinced him to do the film was the script. “It grabbed me right from the first page. Everything just fit together — it was funny, spooky, and very well written,” Dutt said. With its unique storyline, a strong cast, and a fun blend of chills and laughs, The Bhootnii offers something different for audiences and is now streaming only on ZEE5.

title: Parineeti Chopra and Raghav Chadha’s Kapil Sharma Show Shoot Stopped Midway After Raghav’s Mother Falls Ill on Set.
content: Parineeti Chopra and Raghav Chadha, who got married in 2024, have mostly stayed away from the limelight since their wedding. The couple was all set to make a rare joint appearance on The Great Indian Kapil Show, but the shoot had to be stopped midway due to a sudden health scare. According to IANS, Raghav’s mother, who was present on set, began feeling unwell and started shivering unexpectedly. She was immediately taken to a hospital for medical attention, and the filming was halted. The episode was supposed to be a special one, marking Parineeti and Raghav’s first television appearance together. However, due to the emergency, the production team now plans to reschedule the shoot once things settle down. There hasn’t been an official update on Raghav’s mother’s condition yet. Parineeti, who was last seen in Imtiaz Ali’s Amar Singh Chamkila, has been keeping a relatively low profile. She will next be seen in a new Netflix series, which has already completed filming. While the title and release date are still under wraps, the show features an impressive cast including Tahir Raj Bhasin, Jennifer Winget, Harleen Sethi, and Sumeet Vyas.

title: Veteran Filmmaker Chandra Barot Passes Away at 86 After Struggle with Pulmonary Fibrosis.
content: Veteran filmmaker Chandra Barot, best remembered for directing the iconic 1978 film Don starring Amitabh Bachchan, passed away on July 20 at the age of 86. He had been battling pulmonary fibrosis for several years and was admitted to Guru Nanak Hospital, where he breathed his last. His wife, Deepa Barot, confirmed the news of his passing. Barot’s death has left the film industry and his admirers deeply saddened, with tributes pouring in from fans and colleagues across Bollywood. He is celebrated for giving Hindi cinema one of its most memorable thrillers Don, a film that continues to be revered for its stylish storytelling and gripping narrative. Chandra Barot began his career in the 1970s as an assistant director on films like Purab Aur Pachhim, Yaadgar, Shor, and Roti Kapda Aur Makaan. His directorial debut, Don, became a massive box-office hit and earned cult status over the years. He also directed a few Bengali films and other projects, although some were never completed. Despite a limited number of films, Barot’s sharp vision and storytelling left a significant mark on Indian cinema. His legacy lives on through his work, especially the timeless appeal of

title: Mamata Banerjee Sends Best Wishes to Shah Rukh Khan After Injury on King Set: "Praying for His Quick Recovery.
content: Bollywood superstar Shah Rukh Khan reportedly suffered a back injury while shooting an action scene for his upcoming film King. The movie, directed by Siddharth Anand, is especially notable as it stars his daughter, Suhana Khan, in the lead role. West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee expressed concern over his injury, calling him her “brother” and wishing him a speedy recovery in a post on social media. Meanwhile, another highly anticipated film, Saamrajya, is grabbing attention. Directed by Jersey filmmaker Gowtam Tinnanuri, the film features Vijay Deverakonda in a rugged new avatar. He plays a reluctant ruler forced to step up as chaos surrounds him. Tinnanuri describes it as a story of survival, rebirth, and emotional intensity. The film also stars Satyadev and Bhagyashri Borse. Produced by Sithara Entertainments and Fortune Four Cinemas, Saamrajya is set to release in Telugu, Tamil, and Hindi. The teaser has received millions of views, boosted by voiceovers from Jr NTR, Suriya, and Ranbir Kapoor. The film features a powerful score by Anirudh Ravichander and visuals by Girish Gangadharan and Jomon T. John. An AI-designed video has also generated buzz, adding to the excitement around this major 2025 release.

title: Ganga Water Level Rises, Several Districts in Bihar Put on Alert.
content: Patna: Authorities in Bihar have issued an alert for several districts as the water level of the Ganga continues to rise, increasing the risk of flood-like conditions. The warning comes along with a forecast of heavy rainfall in the coming days. Development Commissioner Pratyaya Amrit chaired a high-level meeting on Sunday to assess the readiness of the Disaster Management Department (DMD). According to an official statement, all teams under the DMD have been instructed to remain on high alert due to the rising water levels in the Ganga, as well as expected heavy rain in the catchment areas of Nepal-based rivers like the Gandak and Kosi. Additionally, significant rainfall is predicted in several parts of Bihar, including regions around the Sone river. Officials have been directed to ensure that any signs of flooding are immediately communicated to residents through loudspeakers. In case water begins to enter residential areas, prompt action must be taken to evacuate people to safer locations. The department has also been asked to stay prepared to set up relief camps and community kitchens wherever needed. The state is on alert, and all necessary measures are being taken to minimize risks and ensure public safety during this period of rising water levels and potential flooding.

title: Breaking: Saiyaara draws an unexpectedly strong crowd for Monday morning shows, as YRF breaks the usual trend by offering discounted tickets on Tuesday.
content: Saiyaara is proving to be a major box office success, breaking the usual pattern of weekday slowdowns. Monday morning shows across cities like Mumbai, Jaipur, Ahmedabad, and Bengaluru saw unexpectedly high occupancy, with some early shows nearly sold out. Even in smaller towns like Purnia, Bihar, advance bookings were strong, showing the film’s wide appeal and strong word of mouth. In a surprising move, Yash Raj Films decided to offer discounted tickets on Tuesday Rs. 149 or 199 even though most successful films usually skip this deal to maintain higher earnings. Though the lowest Rs. 99 front-row pricing won’t apply, trade experts have praised YRF for keeping ticket prices accessible. They also noted that Saiyaara would have done well regardless, as fans are eager to watch it without delay. Thanks to this pricing strategy and immense buzz, the film is expected to post double-digit collections on both Monday and Tuesday. Experts believe Saiyaara might even avoid the usual midweek drop, which is rare. The strong weekday footfall has reminded many theatre managers of the pre-pandemic era, marking Saiyaara as a true crowd-puller.

title: PCB faces massive ₹34.8 crore loss as BCCI’s silence adds to Asia Cup uncertainty.
content: The fate of the 2025 Asia Cup remains uncertain, and the Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) could face a major financial blow if the tournament doesn’t go ahead. With no confirmation yet from the Asian Cricket Council (ACC) about the tournament's status, the PCB is preparing for a potential revenue loss, especially after the Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) offered little clarity, reigniting doubts around the event. According to PTI, the PCB had budgeted approximately ₹264 crore in revenue for this year—₹34.8 crore of that expected from the Asia Cup alone. A major chunk of the remaining amount, nearly ₹230 crore, is expected to come from its share of ICC earnings. A board insider emphasized how crucial these two revenue sources are for the financial health of Pakistan cricket. The situation grew more complicated when PCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi, who also heads the ACC, skipped last weekend’s ICC meeting in Singapore and instead joined virtually. CEO Sumair Ahmed represented PCB, but reportedly received little support from BCCI, Sri Lanka, or Afghanistan for the ACC meeting scheduled on July 24 in Dhaka. Multiple boards, including India, refused to travel to Dhaka, making it harder to finalize plans for the tournament. Originally, India was supposed to host the Asia Cup this September, but political tensions after the Pahalgam terror attack in April had already cast doubt on the event. Although Union Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya recently confirmed India’s willingness to play in multination tournaments with Pakistan, tensions remain high especially after Yuvraj Singh and other veterans refused to face Pakistan in the World Championship of Legends, leading to a cancelled match in Birmingham.

title: Ahaan Panday stunned by Karan Johar’s praise for Saiyaara: “I grew up watching Kuch Kuch Hota Hai.
content: Ahaan Panday is riding high on the success of Saiyaara, his debut film that’s making waves at the box office. Adding to the celebration, filmmaker Karan Johar recently praised the film and called Ahaan’s performance “spectacular.” The praise left Ahaan deeply moved, especially since he grew up watching Karan's films. Taking to Instagram, Ahaan reposted Karan’s review and wrote, “I grew up watching Kuch Kuch Hota Hai, I grew up to your school of cinema, and to hear this from you, I am truly shell-shocked. Thank you sir, from the bottom of my heart, a thousand times over.” In his review, Karan called Saiyaara the best film of director Mohit Suri’s career. He praised Ahaan’s emotional performance, saying, “You broke my heart and yet energised me as a filmmaker… your eyes spoke volumes.” He also had kind words for Aneet Padda, calling her “amazing” and noting how her quiet moments and vulnerability added depth to the story.
