He complimented the entire cast and crew, giving a special shoutout to casting director Shanoo Sharma and the editing team. Saiyaara tells the story of Krish Kapoor (Ahaan Panday), a hot-headed musician, and Vaani (Aneet Padda), a reserved aspiring journalist. Their love story struck a chord with audiences, thanks to their natural chemistry and a soundtrack that quickly became a fan favorite. The film is produced by YRF CEO Akshaye Widhani and continues to earn acclaim for its storytelling and performances.

title: Malayalam star once spoke out against powerful filmmaker long before MeToo movement began.
content: From Silk Smitha and National Award-winner Shoba to Bollywood stars like Sushant Singh Rajput and Jiah Khan, Indian cinema has seen several tragic losses actors who allegedly died by suicide. The price of fame often comes at the cost of mental health, and sometimes, even lives. While speculations and conspiracy theories surround many of these cases, the real reasons often remain unknown. One such heartbreaking story is that of Vijayasree, known as the “Marilyn Monroe of Malayalam cinema,” who ended her life at just 21, right when her career was at its peak. In a short span of five years, Vijayasree acted in over 60 films, capturing audiences with her stunning beauty and screen presence. However, despite her potential, she was often typecast as a sex symbol, and her acting talent was rarely given the spotlight it deserved. Even five decades later, the mystery behind her untimely death remains unresolved, with no clear answers about what led her to take such a step. Born on January 8, 1953, in Manacaud, Thiruvananthapuram, to Vasu Pillai and Vijayamma, Vijayasree began her acting journey at just 13 with the Tamil film Chitthi (1966), alongside Padmini and Gemini Ganesan. Offers from Telugu and Kannada films followed, and soon she was noticed by Malayalam cinema. Her first appearance was a small role in Poojapushpam (1969), starring Prem Nazir and Sheela. Her big break came in Rakthapushpam (1970), opposite superstar Prem Nazir and directed by Sasikumar. Their on-screen chemistry was a hit, and Vijayasree quickly rose to stardom, becoming one of the most sought-after actresses of her time.

title: Shilpa Shirodkar recalls false death rumours that left her parents terrified during film shoot.
content: Actor Shilpa Shirodkar recently recalled a shocking incident from her early days in the film industry when a newspaper falsely reported that she had been shot dead. At the time, she was shooting for the film Raghuveer in Kullu Manali with Suniel Shetty. Since mobile phones weren’t common back then, her father desperately tried to reach her at the hotel after reading the disturbing headline. Locals watching the shoot were confused, unsure if it was really Shilpa, since the news of her "death" had already spread. When Shilpa returned to her hotel room, she found nearly 25 missed calls from her panicked father. She later learned that the news was part of a promotional strategy for the film. “There was no PR or media planning back then. I was the last to know about it,” she said. While she admitted the move went too far, she wasn’t angry because the film did well at the box office. Released in 1995, Raghuveer starred Shilpa alongside Suniel Shetty and was a moderate hit. Shilpa made her debut in 1989 with Bhrashtachar and went on to feature in several popular films in the ’90s. After taking a break from films, she returned to the screen through television and recently appeared in Bigg Boss 18 in 2024. Ask ChatGPT

title: ‘A loud bang, then chaos’: Eyewitnesses describe terrifying moments after Bangladesh Air Force jet crash.
content: A Chinese-made F-7 fighter jet belonging to the Bangladesh Air Force crashed into Milestone School and College in Uttara, northern Dhaka, on Monday afternoon, causing widespread devastation. The crash occurred around 1:30 pm, killing at least 18 people, including the pilot, and injuring more than 100 others. One eyewitness, Fahim Hossain, a student at the school, described the horrifying moment: “The jet crashed right in front of my eyes, just 10 feet away. It hit the ground floor of a two-storey building where primary school classes were being held.” The impact caused massive damage to the building. Another student, Rafiqa Taha, who wasn’t on campus at the time, expressed her shock and heartbreak after watching the news. “I was terrified watching videos on TV. My God! It is my school,” said the 16-year-old. Milestone School and College has around 2,000 students from elementary to class 12. The crash has left the entire school community in shock and mourning. As rescue teams worked at the scene, ambulances moved in and out of the school grounds, while anxious parents and family members gathered outside, desperate for news. One father said, “I’ve spoken to my son on the phone, but I still haven’t found him here.” Students who escaped the crash were visibly shaken and in tears, many still trying to process the terrifying experience. Nobel Laureate Muhammad Yunus expressed deep sorrow in a post, calling the loss to the Air Force, students, and school staff “irreparable.”

title: Co-founder faces backlash for sharing honeymoon bed-wetting story as a leadership lesson on LinkedIn.
content: Anthony Karls, a US-based entrepreneur and President of Rocket Clicks, sparked a wave of online reactions after sharing a highly personal story on LinkedIn. In his post, Karls recounted how he accidentally wet the bed during his honeymoon after eating spicy crab soup and becoming dehydrated from a CrossFit workout. What raised eyebrows wasn’t just the story—it was how he tried to turn the embarrassing moment into a leadership lesson. Karls explained that the incident taught him not to take himself too seriously. He linked the experience to leadership by saying that great leaders are those who can laugh at themselves and create an environment where their teams feel safe to take risks. “When you’re willing to look ridiculous, your team feels safe to try bold ideas,” he wrote. He added that being open about failures helps build trust and encourages honesty in the workplace. The post quickly went viral, but not all feedback was positive. Many social media users were confused or amused by the unusual connection between bed-wetting and business leadership. One person joked, “And I thought the Astronomer CEO had a bad day on LinkedIn,” highlighting how the post stood out even in the world of cringe-worthy corporate content. Ask ChatGPT Ask ChatGPT

title: Kalki Koechlin on pregnancy before marriage: "People shamed me, it's such an outdated term.
content: Actor Kalki Koechlin recently opened up about facing judgment for getting pregnant before marriage and the subtle pressure from her family to marry at a young age. She said that while the pressure wasn't overwhelming, it was present, and society made a big deal about her pregnancy, using outdated terms like "out of wedlock." Kalki defended her decision, explaining that she was in a committed relationship and living with her partner, Israeli musician Guy Hershberg. She called out the hypocrisy in society, pointing out that such situations are completely normal but still seen as taboo. She humorously added, “We do sleep together, by the way. We live together, so it happens.” Kalki and Guy welcomed their daughter, Sappho, in 2020 and later got married. She was previously married to filmmaker Anurag Kashyap. Up next, Kalki will be seen in Emma and Angel, an English-language film about a speech-impaired writer trapped after an earthquake, whose only hope is her dog. The release date is yet to be announced.

title: Supreme Court refuses to halt UP's order to display QR codes on Kanwar Yatra route.
content: The Supreme Court on Tuesday declined to stay the Uttar Pradesh government's directive requiring restaurants along the Kanwar Yatra route to display QR codes with the names of their owners. However, the court did order restaurant owners to follow the Food Safety and Standards Act by displaying their license and registration certificates. The matter has been scheduled for another hearing in two weeks to check for compliance. This decision came in response to petitions filed by Trinamool Congress MP Mahua Moitra and academic Apoorvanand Jha, who argued that the QR code rule promotes religious profiling. They pointed out that existing Food Safety regulations do not require QR coding, and cited a previous Supreme Court ruling from July 2024 that stayed a similar Uttar Pradesh Police directive. That earlier ruling stated restaurant owners should not be forced to publicly display names of themselves or their staff. While hearing the petitioners’ concerns, the bench, comprising Justices MM Sundresh and N Kotiswar Singh, said they were not addressing the QR code issue at this stage and suggested it could be challenged in the High Court. Senior lawyer Abhishek Manu Singhvi criticized the move, calling it a divisive policy targeting minority communities and an attack on secular values.

title: Mohit Suri gets emotional as Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda hug on Saiyaara’s final shoot day in unseen BTS video.
content: A touching behind-the-scenes video from the final day of shooting for Saiyaara has surfaced online, showing director Mohit Suri wiping away tears as he wraps up the film. The emotional video, originally shared by assistant cinematographer Ashok Kheer before being taken down, has since been reposted by fans across Instagram and Twitter. In the clip, Mohit stands with the cast and crew as the last scenes are shot, visibly moved while the film’s title track plays in the background. Everyone joins in singing, creating a heartfelt atmosphere. Lead actors Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda, dressed in wedding outfits for the final scene, hug and cheer their director, marking the end of what seems like a deeply personal journey for the entire team. Saiyaara, which released last Friday, has connected strongly with audiences and critics alike. It earned over ₹105 crore by Monday and continues to perform well at the box office. Directed by Mohit Suri and produced by YRF, the film tells the love story of aspiring singer Krish (Ahaan) and journalist-turned-lyricist Vaani (Aneet). With strong performances, a heartfelt story, and praise from stars like Alia Bhatt and Ranveer Singh, Saiyaara is quickly becoming one of the year’s biggest romantic hits. Ask ChatGPT

title: Sham Kaushal got emotional during Om Shanti Om fire scene, felt like he was having a heart attack.
content: Action director Sham Kaushal recently spoke about the intense and emotional experience of filming the climax scene of Om Shanti Om, where Shah Rukh Khan’s character tries to rescue Deepika Padukone’s Shantipriya from a fire. In an interview with Hindi Rush, Sham revealed that the scene was shot without any VFX, and the fire on the set was real. The set was packed with around 100 people, including Shah Rukh, Deepika, three cameramen, and nearly 60 members of the action team. Sham recalled the immense pressure he felt during the shoot, as the fire had to be lit and extinguished within seconds to ensure everyone’s safety. He said the margin for error was so small that even a slight mistake could have turned into a disaster. The responsibility of protecting so many lives weighed heavily on him. He admitted that the stress was overwhelming, and there were moments when he had to step aside and cry on set, fearing something might go wrong. “I felt like I’d have a heart attack,” he said emotionally. The iconic scene remains one of the most memorable moments in the film, but few know the real-life tension and effort that went into making it happen safely.

title: Ajay Devgn says outsiders often think they’ll become stars instantly.
content: While promoting Son of Sardaar 2 on Archana Puran Singh’s YouTube channel, Ajay Devgn opened up about his views on newcomers in the film industry, especially those without a film background. Joined by his co-star Mrunal Thakur, Ajay spoke about how he learned the ins and outs of the industry from his father, the late stunt choreographer Veeru Devgan. He credited his father for teaching him technical skills, dedication, and a strong work ethic, saying that everything he knows about the industry came from him. Archana added that this is why the film industry often prefers people from film families—they grow up around the profession and naturally pick up the discipline and ethics it demands. Ajay agreed and said there's a lot to learn from such an environment. He also pointed out that many newcomers from outside the industry are often misguided. “I’m not talking about everyone,” he clarified, “but many come in not knowing whether they want to be actors or stars. You can’t become a star overnight. First, you have to be an actor. There’s a misunderstanding about how the industry works. In the end, it all comes down to hard work.” Son of Sardaar 2, directed by Vijay Kumar Arora, is set to hit theatres on August 1.

title: PM Modi on Jagdeep Dhankhar's sudden exit: "He had many chances to serve the nation.
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has responded to the sudden resignation of Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar, expressing his good wishes and appreciation for Dhankhar’s service to the nation. In a post on X (formerly Twitter), PM Modi wrote, “Shri Jagdeep Dhankhar Ji has got many opportunities to serve our country in various capacities, including as the Vice President of India. Wishing him good health.” Dhankhar, 74, resigned from his position on Sunday, citing health concerns. In his resignation letter to President Droupadi Murmu, he stated, “To prioritise health care and abide by medical advice, I hereby resign as Vice President of India, effective immediately, in accordance with Article 67(a) of the Constitution.” His resignation came unexpectedly, especially as it was the opening day of the Monsoon Session of Parliament. Dhankhar’s term had started in August 2022 and was supposed to continue until August 2027. Along with being Vice President, he also served as Chairman of the Rajya Sabha. His sudden departure has sparked speculation in political and media circles, though no specific details about his medical condition were shared in his letter.

title: Shastri criticizes Jadeja, says “If he had even 40% of Stokes’ self-belief, he’d be a different player.
content: Former India head coach Ravi Shastri has shared strong views on Ravindra Jadeja's performance during India's narrow loss to England at Lord’s. Though Jadeja played a gritty knock of 61 not out and built crucial partnerships with the lower order, Shastri believes he missed a chance to push harder for a win. India, having collapsed to 112/8, eventually reached 172, giving fans a glimmer of hope before losing by 22 runs. While Jadeja held one end and played cautiously, Shastri felt he should’ve taken more risks earlier in the innings, especially when the field was up and the ball was still hard. Speaking on The Overlap Podcast, Shastri said the best time to attack was before lunch when Jadeja and Nitish Reddy were batting together. He explained that once the ball got soft, chances of a surprise dismissal increased, so a more aggressive approach earlier could’ve helped reduce the target and kept India in the game longer. Drawing comparisons to Ben Stokes’ iconic 2019 Ashes knock at Headingley, Shastri pointed out that Jadeja has the talent but needs to believe more in his batting. “If Jadeja had even 40% of Stokes' belief, he’d win more games. He has the skill, but he must take charge,” Shastri said. Ask ChatGPT

title: Pathan criticizes Bumrah with sharp comment: "Either give it your all or take a rest.
content: Former Indian all-rounder Irfan Pathan has strongly criticized how Jasprit Bumrah’s workload is being managed, saying that when you're playing for India, you need to give your absolute best. Speaking on his YouTube channel, Pathan pointed out that playing for the country also means playing for your teammates, and there’s no room for holding back during crucial moments. Bumrah, who has been closely monitored by the team management since his back injury on the Australia tour, is being used carefully in the current series, with the captain and coach deciding he will play only three of the five Test matches. He's also been given shorter spells to manage his workload. However, Pathan believes this cautious approach could be limiting Bumrah’s impact. He explained that there shouldn't be a hard rule about bowling only a certain number of overs. “If you’ve bowled five overs and someone like Joe Root walks in, you can’t say you’re done just because your spell is over. Either you give it your all or take proper rest,” Pathan said. Despite these concerns, Bumrah remains a key player, having taken two five-wicket hauls in the series and continuing to be a major threat for England. Ask ChatGPT

title: Saiyaara Becomes 12th Highest First Tuesday Grosser, Surpassing Dangal and Pathaan.
content: Saiyaara’s success continues to grow, with the Mohit Suri-directed film starring newcomers Ahaan Panday and Aneet Padda reaching a significant milestone. On its fifth day in theaters, the film earned Rs. 25 crore, making it the 12th highest first Tuesday grosser of all time. This achievement puts it ahead of blockbuster hits like Dangal, Pathaan, and Tiger Zinda Hai. What makes Saiyaara’s performance even more impressive is that it has accomplished this without the backing of major stars, franchise support, or a holiday release. Unlike many other films on the list, Saiyaara relies on strong storytelling and music, proving that fresh talent and emotionally driven content can compete with big-budget Bollywood hits and pan-India blockbusters. With total net collections of Rs. 133.75 crore in India and steady box office numbers expected, Saiyaara is carving out a place as one of 2025’s major successes. Its presence alongside films like Baahubali, War, and Jawan shows that audiences are eager for original stories and new faces, highlighting Mohit Suri’s direction and the film’s emotional appeal.

title: Director Amit Rai Confirms Shahid Kapoor’s Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Film Has Been Shelved.
content: Shahid Kapoor’s film on Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj has been officially shelved, director Amit Rai confirmed in a recent interview. Known for OMG 2, Amit expressed frustration over the film industry’s harsh system, where even successful directors face constant challenges. Amit shared that despite making a Rs 180 crore film, the system’s casting and management hurdles make it difficult to work. He also mentioned that actors like Pankaj Tripathi and Akshay Kumar showed interest in his upcoming project, praising their honesty compared to others who prefer safer, commercial roles. Meanwhile, Shahid Kapoor is busy filming Vishal Bhardwaj’s project, set to release this December. Despite competition from Ranveer Singh’s Dhurandhar and Prabhas’ The Raja

title: Param Sundari release delayed to avoid clash with Metro and Saiyaara.
content: The makers of Param Sundari, starring Sidharth Malhotra and Janhvi Kapoor, have decided to postpone the film’s release to give it a better chance at standing out. Originally set for July 25, the release was first delayed to avoid clashing with Son of Sardaar 2. However, insiders now reveal the move was also to steer clear of a crowded schedule filled with several romantic films releasing around the same time. With two major romantic dramas Anurag Basu’s Metro…In Dino and Mohit Suri’s Saiyaara—coming out shortly after, along with Dhadak 2 hitting theaters on August 1, the makers felt Param Sundari needed some breathing room to avoid being lost among similar love stories. The film is now expected to release on either August 29 or September 5, a date that should give it better visibility at the box office. Directed by Tushar Jalota, known for Dasvi, Param Sundari is described as a heartfelt cross-cultural love story between a young man from North India and a woman from South India. The film marks a fresh pairing of Sidharth Malhotra and Janhvi Kapoor and promises a warm, contemporary romance under the banner of Maddock Films. To make the most of the delay, the team plans to shoot a new promotional song once Sidharth finishes his current projects and adjusts to fatherhood. Fans are eagerly awaiting this fresh and promising on-screen duo.

title: Ben Stokes Criticizes ICC Over World Test Championship Points Penalty, Citing ‘Asia Reference.
content: Ben Stokes has issued a firm warning to India ahead of the fourth Test at Old Trafford, making it clear that England will not back down from any on-field confrontations. In a pre-match press conference, Stokes highlighted the intensity of the series, with England leading 2-1, saying, “It’s a massive series and heat will be shown. Have England been nice? Potentially. We won’t purposely start anything, but we won’t take any backward step.” He also shared that the team enjoyed a well-needed break after their win at Lord’s, joking about feeling like he was in a long-distance relationship with his family during the rest. Regarding the slow over-rate penalties faced by England, Stokes defended the team’s performance at Lord’s by explaining the challenges on the field. He pointed out that injuries to spinner Shoaib Bashir meant England had to rely heavily on fast bowlers on the final day, which naturally slowed the game. “We played five days, that was our 15th day of cricket. With Bashir injured, we couldn’t use our spinner much and had to throw seamers for most of the day, which slows things down,” he said. Stokes also suggested that over-rate rules should consider different bowling styles and conditions, explaining that spinners’ overs typically take less time than those of seamers. He emphasized the physical toll on fast bowlers, adding that the current system doesn’t always reflect the reality on the field.

title: India reacts to UK media report about mix-up of victim’s body after Air India crash.
content: The Indian government has responded to a UK report alleging that the family of a British victim from the Air India flight 171 crash was mistakenly given the wrong body. Officials stated that they are working closely with British authorities to address these concerns. The tragic crash of the Boeing 787 near Ahmedabad on June 12 resulted in 242 fatalities on board and 19 deaths on the ground, including 52 British citizens. According to the Daily Mail, mistakes were made during the identification and repatriation of some victims, with one family reportedly abandoning funeral plans after being told their loved one’s coffin contained the remains of an unidentified passenger. In another instance, remains from more than one victim were mixed and had to be separated before burial. India’s External Affairs Ministry emphasized that all procedures were followed with professionalism and respect for the deceased. Both countries have launched high-level inquiries to investigate the issue, and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to discuss these concerns with Prime Minister Narendra Modi during his visit to the UK later this month. The visit will also include meetings with King Charles III and the signing of a bilateral free trade agreement.

title: Deepika Padukone left Sandeep Reddy Vanga’s Spirit for reasons unrelated to working hours.
content: However, for the first time here is the truth on why Deepika actually walked out of Vanga’s film Spirit starring Prabhas. From a source very close to the development here is the truth. “Deepika loved the script and role in Sandeep Vanga’s film. The contract was being drawn up, when a week later she was offered Atlee’s film with Allu Arjun which she liked even more than Sandeep Vanga’s film. Since she couldn’t do both the projects, she decided to opt out of Spirit, citing differences of opinion on fee, work hours, etc,” said the source. The truth is, Vanga had no objections to whatever misgivings Deepika expressed on the project. She just got an offer that she liked better and changed her mind about Spirit with Prabhas. And no harm in that. She had already worked with Prabhas in Kalki 2898 AD. Allu Arjun is going to be a new experience for her.

title: Tanushree Dutta breaks down in emotional video, alleges harassment at Mumbai home: “I’m sick and tired.
content: Actress Tanushree Dutta has once again raised concerns about her safety, sharing a deeply emotional video on social media where she breaks down in tears. In the clip, now widely circulated, Tanushree pleads for help, claiming that she has been facing continuous harassment at her Mumbai residence since 2018. She wrote in the caption, “I’m sick and tired of this harassment!! It’s been going on since 2018 #MeToo. Aaj fed up hokar maine police ko call kiya... Please someone help me! Do something before it’s too late.” Tanushree, who gained fame with films like Aashiq Banaya Aapne, had sparked India’s #MeToo movement by accusing Nana Patekar of sexual harassment during a film shoot in 2018. Though the case was later closed, she has maintained that the trauma and stress never stopped. In a recent post, she also shared an audio clip, claiming loud banging and disturbing noises outside her home have been a regular occurrence since 2020. Despite numerous complaints to her building management, she says no action has been taken. The actress revealed that her health has worsened, suffering from Chronic Fatigue Syndrome due to constant stress. She has appealed for urgent intervention, warning that her situation is becoming unbearable and dangerous.

title: Supreme Court says High Court misused its discretion in granting bail in Renukaswamy murder case.
content: New Delhi: The Supreme Court on Thursday criticized the Karnataka High Court’s decision to grant bail to Kannada actor Darshan Thoogudeepa and six others in the Renukaswamy murder case. Calling it a “perverse exercise of discretionary power,” the court questioned whether the High Court had used proper judgment. A bench of Justices J.B. Pardiwala and R. Mahadevan was hearing the Karnataka government’s appeal against the High Court’s December 13, 2024, order granting bail. The court noted that all accused are now out on bail and the trial has not even started. Justice Pardiwala expressed concern that the High Court's bail order seemed more like an acquittal, questioning whether such detailed reasoning is normal in bail matters. He also criticized how the High Court dismissed the testimony of two eyewitnesses as unreliable without thorough reasoning. The court reserved its verdict for now and asked remaining defence lawyers to submit their notes within a week. Earlier, on July 17, the same bench had already expressed doubts about the High Court’s judgment, stating they were “not at all convinced” by how the discretion was used to grant bail.

title: FIR filed against Prashant Kishor and Jan Suraaj workers over Bihar protest.
content: The Patna police have registered an FIR against Jan Suraaj leader Prashant Kishor and around 300 party workers following a protest near the Bihar Assembly that turned violent on Wednesday. The demonstration, which was held without official permission, was aimed at raising issues such as unemployment, land rights, and alleged corruption in the land survey process. Kishor had earlier announced plans to gherao the Assembly. As the protestors, including party spokesperson Vivek and state president Manoj Bharti, marched toward the Assembly, police intervened at Chitkohra roundabout to stop them. A clash broke out when protestors attempted to force their way through the barricades. Despite repeated warnings, the crowd did not disperse, prompting police to resort to lathi-charge. Several protestors were reportedly injured. The FIR was lodged at Sachivalaya police station under sections 190, 191(2), 132, and 223 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita. Police accused the protestors of unlawful assembly, inciting public unrest, and violating prohibitory orders. City SP (Central) Diksha confirmed the FIR was based on a statement by the deputed magistrate. Authorities are now reviewing CCTV and video footage to identify individuals responsible for the violence and further legal action. Ask ChatGPT

title: Aamir Khan to Hoist National Flag at 2025 Indian Film Festival of Melbourne.
content: Renowned actor and filmmaker Aamir Khan will hoist the Indian national flag during the Indian Film Festival of Melbourne (IFFM) 2025 as part of the Independence Day celebrations this August. The flag hoisting has become a key tradition of the festival, symbolizing unity and pride for the Indian community abroad. The event brings together people from different backgrounds including Indian and Australian communities to honour India’s heritage and celebrate its cinema. Supported by the Government of Victoria, IFFM is the largest Indian film festival held outside of India. Each year, it showcases a wide range of films across languages and regions and hosts prominent figures from the film industry. Aamir Khan’s participation this year adds a special significance to the ceremony. Known for films that strike a balance between mainstream appeal and social relevance, his presence reflects the festival’s commitment to meaningful and inclusive storytelling. Festival Director Mitu Bhowmick Lange expressed how emotional the flag ceremony is, especially when done abroad. She described it as a unifying experience filled with pride and honour. IFFM 2025 will screen over 75 films in 31 languages and include panel discussions, masterclasses, and cultural events to promote global connections through cinema.

title: Anupam Kher’s Tanvi The Great declared tax-free in Delhi after Madhya Pradesh.
content: Following its tax-free status in Madhya Pradesh, Anupam Kher’s film Tanvi The Great has now been declared tax-free in Delhi. The announcement was made by Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta, who praised the film’s powerful message of inclusion and determination. Sharing the news on social media, she wrote, “Tanvi The Great is an inspiring story of a young, special girl who overcomes all odds to pursue her dreams. The film’s message aligns with our commitment to promote stories that awaken patriotism and a sense of national service.” The film tells the story of Tanvi, a young autistic girl who dreams of joining the Indian Army like her late father. Played by Shubhangi Dutt, Tanvi’s character has touched hearts across the country. The film also stars Anupam Kher, Boman Irani, Jackie Shroff, Arvind Swami, Karan Tacker, Nasser, and British actor Iain Glen. Chief Minister Gupta, after watching a special screening, said the film was deeply emotional and should be seen by every child. She expressed plans to screen it for students across Delhi. Produced by Anupam Kher Studios and NFDC, the film has received international acclaim at major film festivals including Cannes, London, and New York.

title: Janhvi Kapoor reacts strongly to Kalyan assault video, asks “What kind of upbringing is this.
content: Bollywood actress Janhvi Kapoor has expressed outrage over a disturbing incident in Kalyan, where a man was caught on video violently assaulting a hospital receptionist. The viral video shows the accused, Gokul Jha, dragging the woman by her hair after she allegedly refused to let him skip the queue. The victim, who suffered serious injuries, is currently being treated in a Dombivli hospital. The police have taken Jha into custody. Reacting to the incident, Janhvi shared her anger through an Instagram story. “This man belongs in jail,” she wrote. “What gives anyone the right to behave this way? What kind of upbringing leads to such inhumanity without any guilt or remorse?” She also questioned society’s response, saying, “Shame on us for not dealing with this kind of violence more aggressively. Nothing justifies this.” Her strong words resonated widely, sparking fresh conversations about violence against women and the importance of justice. Janhvi’s willingness to speak out reflects her growing influence beyond films. Professionally, Janhvi has several upcoming projects, including Sunny Sanskari Ki Tulsi Kumari with Varun Dhawan and Param Sundari with Siddharth Malhotra. She also debuted at the 2025 Cannes Film Festival for her international film Homebound. Ask ChatGPT

title: ED raids 35 locations, 50 companies linked to Anil Ambani’s Reliance Group in loan fraud investigation.
content: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) carried out raids at around 35 locations on Thursday as part of a money laundering investigation involving Anil Ambani’s Reliance Anil Dhirubhai Ambani Group. The action is connected to alleged loan fraud involving Reliance Home Finance Ltd (RHFL) and Reliance Commercial Finance Ltd (RCFL), with officials saying over 50 firms and 25 individuals are under scrutiny. Former Yes Bank chairman Rana Kapoor is also under investigation. The ED is acting on two CBI FIRs filed in September 2022, which alleged irregularities in loans granted by Yes Bank to RHFL and RCFL. The investigation has since expanded with inputs from agencies like SEBI, the National Housing Bank, NFRA, and Bank of Baroda. According to officials, around ₹3,000 crore in loans may have been illegally diverted between 2017 and 2019. Evidence suggests the loans were disbursed without proper checks, sometimes even before official approval, and often to shell companies with weak financials or links to the promoter group. In response, Reliance Infrastructure and Reliance Power said the allegations relate to old transactions and that RCom is already under insolvency proceedings, while RHFL has been resolved as per a Supreme Court ruling.

title: CM Mamata Banerjee urges more use of Bengali in films and TV shows
content: Kolkata: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has urged filmmakers and TV producers to promote the use of Bengali in their work, stressing the importance of preserving the state’s language and cultural identity. Speaking at the ‘Mahanayak Samman’ ceremony on the death anniversary of legendary actor Uttam Kumar, Banerjee said every language is beautiful, but it is vital to give priority to one's mother tongue. “While we embrace the richness of various cultures across India, it’s equally important for each state to protect and cherish its own language and traditions,” she said. “We must ensure our younger generation values and respects our heritage.” Addressing the television industry specifically, the Chief Minister encouraged the inclusion of Bengali songs in serials that often rely heavily on Hindi tracks. “Why not highlight the immense talent we have in Bengal? Our lyricists and composers create beautiful music that deserves to be heard,” she said. Banerjee emphasized that Bengali, with its emotional depth and cultural richness, should be more visible in mainstream media. Her appeal was both a cultural reminder and a call to action—urging creators to take pride in Bengal’s legacy while continuing to welcome diversity. Ask ChatGPT

title: Daboo Malik responds to son Amaal Mallik’s claim that Anu Malik ruined his career: “He shouldn’t have said that.
content: Music composer Amaal Mallik recently made headlines when he accused his uncle Anu Malik of sabotaging his father Daboo Malik’s career, claiming Anu would often take away projects by offering to work for free or for less money. Amaal also said that other composers like Sajid-Wajid had a role in sidelining his father and that Daboo was too innocent at the time to fight back. Now, Daboo Malik has reacted to his son’s comments in an interview with Moneycontrol, saying the remarks were unnecessary and came from a personal place. Daboo explained that sometimes, the struggles shared over family dinners stick in children’s minds, and years later, they form strong opinions without knowing the full picture. He said Amaal’s perspective wasn’t entirely accurate and that it wasn’t needed to publicly call out Anu Malik. Daboo acknowledged the industry is competitive and everyone looks out for themselves—no one is expected to hold your hand or make you a star. While he agreed that there were tough times, he defended Anu’s hard work and said he holds no grudges. “Everyone has their own journey,” he said, adding that he is a happy father watching his children succeed.

title: Sonu Sood helps late actor Fish Venkat’s family with ₹1.5 lakh support.
content: Telugu actor and comedian Venkat Raj, known by fans as Fish Venkat, sadly passed away on July 18 in Hyderabad due to kidney-related complications. He was 53 and had been receiving dialysis treatment in the ICU. His family had been trying to raise around ₹50 lakh for a kidney transplant, but unfortunately, he passed away before the funds could be arranged. Venkat was a familiar face in Telugu cinema, having acted in films like Adhurs, Gabbar Singh, Shivam, and Khaidi No. 150. His last role was in the 2024 movie Coffee with a Killer. After the news of his passing broke, actor Sonu Sood stepped in to help the grieving family. He sent ₹1.5 lakh as financial support and also called Venkat’s wife and other relatives to offer his condolences and promised to be there for them if they needed anything. Sonu reportedly thought Venkat was still under treatment and was shocked to hear of his death. Venkat’s wife said the family was comforted by Sonu’s gesture, especially since Venkat had always admired him. While there were rumors that actor Prabhas donated ₹50 lakh, the family clarified they never received any money and that the call they got was from an impersonator. Other stars like Pawan Kalyan and Vishwak Sen also reportedly offered help, though the aid came late. Still, Sonu Sood’s support brought some comfort to the family during this tough time.

title: Abhishek Banerjee starts shooting for his first Tamil film in Chennai, says it feels like coming home.
content: Abhishek Banerjee recently shared a post from Chennai on Instagram, sparking interest not just because he's working on a new project, but also because the city holds a special place in his heart. Although he was born in Kharagpur, he grew up in Kalpakkam, a coastal town near Chennai, and spent much of his childhood there. So, returning to Tamil Nadu for a shoot feels deeply personal to him. He’s currently in Chennai filming a new, yet-to-be-announced project, right at a time when his acting career is gaining momentum. His performance in Paatal Lok continues to draw praise, and Stolen has been creating a buzz, especially among Tamil audiences. His increasing popularity in the South adds more significance to this moment. For Abhishek, being back in Chennai feels like coming home. A source close to the actor shared that the city brings back many childhood memories — from the local food to the smell of the sea and the rhythm of monsoon rains. Although the new project’s details are still under wraps, the experience of shooting in a place that once shaped his early life has made this journey special for both him and his team.

title: Neeraj vs Arshad clash in doubt as Nadeem may skip Poland Diamond League due to injury.
content: Neeraj Chopra and Arshad Nadeem were expected to face off for the first time since the 2024 Paris Olympics at the upcoming Poland Diamond League. However, that much-anticipated clash may not happen. Arshad recently underwent calf surgery, and his participation is now uncertain. At the Paris Olympics, Arshad stunned everyone with a throw of 92.97m to win gold, while Neeraj secured silver with a best of 89.45m. The two are also scheduled to compete in Switzerland next month, but Arshad’s coach has said that he may skip both events to focus on recovering fully ahead of the World Athletics Championships in Tokyo this September. “He had been struggling with a calf issue for a while, so the decision was made to go for surgery. Right now, he’s focused on rehab in London and preparing for the World Championships,” his coach told PTI. He added that Arshad might compete in just one event before the Tokyo showdown. Meanwhile, Neeraj, who recently crossed the 90m mark, is working on improving his consistency. He revealed that he tends to lean too far on his left side during throws in competition, something he's now working to correct for more stable performances.

title: Bigg Boss 19 promo out: Salman Khan teases the show's return with a new logo. Watch here.
content: Fans of Bigg Boss can finally rejoice — the wait for Season 19 is almost over! The popular reality show, hosted by Salman Khan for several years, is making a comeback soon. A new promo for the season was recently released by JioCinema (formerly JioHotstar), giving a glimpse of the show’s new logo and hinting at the upcoming chaos. The promo was shared on Instagram by both JioCinema and JioCinema Reality, with the caption: “Countdown hogaya hai shuru, hoga chaos unlock soon! Stay tuned!” This marks the first official announcement for the new season, which will also be available on the Colors TV channel. The show is expected to premiere on the weekend of August 29–30, with episodes dropping on JioCinema before being aired on television. As per reports, Salman Khan will return as the host initially, but after three months, other big names like Farah Khan, Karan Johar, and Anil Kapoor might step in to host the show. The new season is expected to feature 15 contestants along with 3–5 wild card entries. Over 20 celebrities and influencers have reportedly been approached to join the upcoming season of Bigg Boss 19

title: 25 OTT platforms banned; officials cite Ullu’s reality show 'House Arrest' with Ajaz Khan as example.
content: The Indian government has banned 25 OTT platforms, including Ullu, ALTT, and Hulchul, over content it described as "pornographic in nature." One of the key examples cited by officials was Ullu’s controversial reality show House Arrest, hosted by actor Ajaz Khan. Although House Arrest was removed from Ullu in May 2025 following intervention by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, officials said the platform continued to share obscene and, in some cases, pornographic material. According to sources, a warning had already been sent to several OTT platforms back in September 2024 regarding similar issues. The Digital Publisher Content Grievances Council (DPCGC), a self-regulatory body led by a former Supreme Court judge, also took action against Ullu. It had previously asked the platform to take down over 100 web series. However, sources claimed that Ullu would temporarily remove or edit the content, only to later repost it in its original form—essentially bypassing warnings. House Arrest came under fire earlier this year after Ajaz Khan asked inappropriate questions about sex positions to a female contestant and had others act them out on camera. Another viral clip showed women being asked to remove their undergarments on camera as part of a "challenge." Both Khan and Ullu founder Vibhu Agarwal were summoned by the National Commission for Women. Public outrage also followed, with one user tagging Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw and calling the show “disgusting” and “vulgar,” demanding an immediate ban.

title: Ravi Shastri slams Shubman Gill, says he doesn’t trust two of his own teammates.
content: Shubman Gill’s captaincy came under fire after he delayed bringing Washington Sundar into the attack during England’s first innings in the fourth Test at Manchester. Despite England reaching 306 for 2 with Joe Root and Ollie Pope well set, Sundar — one of India’s two spinners — was not given the ball until the 68th over. This decision puzzled many, especially since Sundar had taken 4 wickets for just 22 runs in the previous match at Lord’s. Former India coach Ravi Shastri criticised Gill for not trusting his own players, particularly spinners like Sundar and Jadeja. On commentary, Shastri pointed out that if two spinners are picked, the captain must back them. Even though Sundar bowled just four overs before lunch, he showed promise, troubling Pope with drift, suggesting his introduction came too late to make a real impact. India’s bowling unit, including Jasprit Bumrah, struggled to maintain pressure. Bumrah was inconsistent, often bowling on the pads, which allowed Pope and Root to score freely. Debutant Anshul Kamboj created a chance, but wicketkeeper Dhruv Jurel couldn’t grab a tough edge. Overall, India’s lack of trust in spin and inconsistent pace bowling allowed England to seize control of the match.

title: PM Modi says India-Maldives friendship will stay strong and transparent.
content: During his visit to the Maldives, Prime Minister Narendra Modi emphasized the strong bond between India and the island nation, saying, “For us, it is always friendship first.” Speaking alongside Maldivian President Mohamed Muizzu, PM Modi highlighted the close ties and deep-rooted relationship between the two countries, describing India as the Maldives' closest neighbour. He noted that while India and the Maldives are celebrating 60 years of formal diplomatic ties, their connection is much older and deeper, comparing it to the depth of the sea. He also mentioned the release of commemorative stamps featuring traditional boats from both nations, symbolizing their shared heritage and journey as not just neighbours but partners. Modi assured continued Indian support for strengthening the Maldives’ defence and announced a $565 million line of credit. He said talks have begun on a Free Trade Agreement and plans are in place to finalize a Bilateral Investment Treaty soon. He also spoke about the local currency settlement system, allowing direct trade between the Indian Rupee and the Maldivian Rufiyaa. Highlighting the growing use of India’s UPI system in the Maldives, he said it will boost sectors like tourism and retail. As a mark of respect, the Maldives National Defence Force presented him with the traditional 'Haiykolhu'.

title: Ekta Kapoor Not Linked to ALTT, Says Balaji After OTT Ban.
content: After the Indian government ordered a ban on 24 OTT platforms for allegedly streaming explicit content, one of the platforms mentioned was ALTT (formerly ALTBalaji). In response to media speculation, Balaji Telefilms issued a clear statement on July 26, distancing producer Ekta Kapoor and her mother Shobha Kapoor from the platform. Balaji Telefilms clarified that it is a professionally managed media company listed on the BSE and NSE, and that ALTT is now entirely operated by them. This comes after the official merger of ALT Digital Media Entertainment Ltd. with Balaji Telefilms, which was completed on June 20, 2025. The statement addressed reports claiming that ALTT had been taken down by authorities and was still connected to the Kapoors. Balaji strongly denied this, stating that both Ekta and Shobha Kapoor stepped down from all roles in the company back in June 2021 and have had no involvement with ALTT since then. They also urged media houses to avoid spreading misinformation and to report accurately. The clarification follows the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting’s recent directive asking internet providers to block 24 OTT platforms accused of distributing vulgar or sexually explicit content.

title: Varun Dhawan Completes 6-Day Shoot for Thama; Vampire vs Bhediya Clash Teased.
content: The Maddock Horror Comedy Cinematic Universe has become a major hit, especially after the blockbuster success of Stree 2 (2024), which earned over ₹500 crores at the box office. Now, all eyes are on the next film in the franchise, Thama, starring Ayushmann Khurrana, Rashmika Mandanna, Nawazuddin Siddiqui, and Paresh Rawal. Directed by Aditya Sarpotdar of Munjya fame, the film also features Varun Dhawan reprising his role as Bhediya. According to reports, Varun has a much larger role this time, with six days of shooting indicating his strong presence in the story. In Thama, Ayushmann plays a vampire, setting the stage for an epic clash with Varun’s werewolf character. The vampire vs. werewolf rivalry is a classic trope, and producer Dinesh Vijan is said to have gone all out with the scale and VFX of their action scenes. Shot between March and April this year, the showdown promises to be a visual spectacle. The film is expected to not only thrill audiences but also lay the groundwork for Bhediya 2, another installment in the growing horror-comedy universe. Thama is scheduled for release during Diwali 2025 and marks Ayushmann Khurrana’s first film after more than two years, his last being Dream Girl 2 in August 2023. Meanwhile, Varun Dhawan will appear in Sunny Sanskari Ki Tulsi Kumari with Janhvi Kapoor before Thama hits screens. Other upcoming titles in the Maddock universe, like Shakti Shalini and Chamunda, are expected to get new release dates soon due to delays. Ask ChatGPT

title: PM Modi Tops Global Leader Approval Ratings; Trump Misses Top 5.
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has once again topped the global list of most popular democratic leaders, according to the latest survey by US-based Morning Consult. With a 75% approval rating, Modi is ranked as the world’s most trusted leader. The survey, conducted between July 4 and 10, 2025, found that three out of every four respondents had a positive opinion of his leadership, while 18% disapproved and 7% remained undecided. South Korean President Lee Jae Myung, who took office just a month ago, secured the second spot with a 59% approval rating. Despite being new to the role, he received strong support, though 29% of people surveyed disapproved of his leadership and 13% were unsure. The list includes only leaders of democratic countries and is based on rolling daily interviews conducted by Morning Consult across various nations. US President Donald Trump ranked eighth on the list with a 44% approval score, lower than expected considering his recent election victory. Some analysts believe his policy decisions may have affected his popularity. BJP leaders, including Amit Malviya and Union Minister Sarbananda Sonowal, celebrated Modi’s top ranking, calling it a sign of strong and respected leadership on the global stage.

title: Zeenat Aman Shares Memes About Herself, Says She’s Embracing the Lighter Side After a Serious Month.
content: Veteran actress Zeenat Aman recently opened up about her absence from Instagram, revealing that it had been a tough month at home. In a heartfelt yet light-hearted post, she shared that the Aman-Khan household had gone through a “woefully serious” time, which is why she had stayed off social media. While she didn’t go into details, she said she’s now ready to embrace a lighter mood and, in that spirit, decided to post a few fun memes featuring herself. Zeenat mentioned that she stumbled upon these “tongue-in-cheek” memes online, some of which also featured iconic actors Dev Anand and Prem Chopra. Calling memes the “internet’s love language,” she said she wanted to bring a smile to people’s faces and encouraged her followers to pick their favorite. She added that while she’s staying in this weekend, she hopes others are out having fun - and enjoying the laughs she’s shared. The post drew warm reactions from fans and fellow celebrities. Celina Jaitly commented on how much she loved the memes, while Tanuja Chandra praised the one featuring Prem Chopra and offered kind words, hoping Zeenat’s difficult days would pass soon. Fans also admired her for being able to laugh at herself and spread joy even during challenging times. Zeenat will next be seen in the film Bun Tikki, alongside Shabana Azmi and Abhay Deol, produced by Manish Malhotra’s Stage5 Productions.

title: Chirag Paswan Defends Bihar SIR, Says It's Happened Before as INDIA Bloc Alleges Voter Removal.
content: The ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls in Bihar has sparked a major political row, with the INDIA bloc accusing the government of trying to remove large numbers of legitimate voters. They claim the process could lead to the deletion of thousands possibly lakhs of names from the voter list. Union Minister Chirag Paswan defended the move, saying the process isn’t new and has been carried out four times before in the same manner. Speaking to ANI, he emphasized that the revision is being done fairly and added, “No injustice will be done to any citizen, but if someone is an infiltrator, they won’t be allowed to misuse the right to vote.” Despite Paswan’s assurances, tensions continue to rise. Opposition leaders like Rabri Devi, Rahul Gandhi, and Priyanka Gandhi protested inside Parliament, accusing the government of using the SIR to suppress voters. The matter led to repeated disruptions in both the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha, causing adjournments for the fourth straight day of the Monsoon Session. Outside Parliament, criticism also grew louder. Shatrughan Sinha questioned the motive behind the revision, comparing it to alleged voter manipulation in Maharashtra. Trinamool MP Mahua Moitra went further, accusing the Election Commission of acting like an extension of the BJP—something she said has never happened before in India's history. Ask ChatGPT

title: Woman in Bihar Faints During Home Guard Recruitment, Later Gang-Raped in Ambulance.
content: A 26-year-old woman fainted during a race held as part of the home guard recruitment drive in Bodh Gaya, Bihar. While she was being taken to the hospital in an ambulance, she was allegedly gang-raped by the ambulance driver and a technician. Police arrested both suspects Vinay Kumar from Utren village in Gaya, who was driving the ambulance, and Ajit Kumar from Chandanpur village in Nalanda. The ambulance had been stationed at the recruitment venue for emergencies, but the victim’s family raised concerns that there was no female medical staff on board, despite the event being exclusively for women candidates. The incident came to light after a case was registered at the Bodh Gaya police station under Section 70(1) of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita. The woman’s statement was recorded, and she underwent a medical examination at Anugrah Narayan Magadh Medical College and Hospital. Senior Superintendent of Police (SSP) Anand Kumar confirmed that a special investigation team, led by City SP Ramanand Kumar Kaushal, has been formed to look into the case. The accused were identified through CCTV footage and the victim's account and were arrested within hours. SSP Kumar assured that the investigation would be completed swiftly, and a charge sheet will be filed for a speedy trial to ensure justice.

title: Nasser Hussain Mocks: After Gill’s 269, People Forgot Kohli and Rohit.
content: Shubman Gill’s debut series as India’s Test captain has become a challenging experience. After starting strong with two centuries and a double ton, Gill's form and decisions as captain have dipped in the last two matches, drawing criticism—especially during the Manchester Test. One key point of criticism has been his delayed use of Washington Sundar, a spinner who could have made an impact earlier on a turning pitch. Despite Sundar’s success in the previous Test at Lord’s, Gill didn’t bring him into the attack until the 60th over. When Sundar finally bowled, he picked up two quick wickets, but England had already built a massive total of 669. Former England captain Nasser Hussain offered advice, urging Gill to block out social media and focus on learning. “When Gill scored 269, people were asking ‘Who is Kohli? Who is Rohit?’ Now, after two bad games, every decision is questioned,” said Hussain, highlighting the fickle nature of public opinion. England, led by big knocks from Joe Root (150), Ben Stokes (141), and openers Crawley and Duckett, dominated the Indian bowlers. With England in full control, India now needs a miracle from their batters to save the series.

title: Aneet Padda’s Upcoming Film Nyaya to Stream Before Her Big-Screen Debut in Saiyaara.
content: This morning, social media lit up with buzz around Aneet Padda’s next project after Saiyaara. Reports claimed that the Gen Z breakout star would be seen next in a streaming film directed by Nitya Mehra and her husband Karan Kapadia. However, industry insiders clarified that the project, titled Nyaya, was actually filmed before Aneet signed on for Saiyaara. According to a source quoted by Bollywood Hungama, Aneet is firmly positioned as a big-screen star for YRF. “Nyaya was shot before Saiyaara and won’t impact her future as a theatrical lead. She’s 22, on the verge of delivering a ₹400 crore blockbuster, and is being seen as a true Gen Z icon. The studio has major plans to shape her as the face of a generation, and that can only happen by focusing on theatrical releases,” the source said. As for Saiyaara, the Mohit Suri-directed film also marking Ahaan Panday’s debut—has already crossed the ₹200 crore mark in India within just nine days of its release.

title: Deepika Padukone joins The Shift’s Global Impact List with Selena Gomez and others.
content: Global cultural platform The Shift has unveiled its latest list celebrating over 90 influential women from around the world, including names like Amal Clooney, Mariska Hargitay, Selena Gomez, Billie Eilish, Angelina Jolie, Amanda Gorman, Jessica Chastain, Olivia Rodrigo, Lucy Liu, Misty Copeland, Billie Jean King and India’s very own Deepika Padukone. Known not just for her impact on cinema but also for her work in mental health and women’s empowerment through her Live Love Laugh Foundation, Deepika’s inclusion marks a major global recognition. Sharing her excitement on social media, Deepika wrote, “In tribute to the one and only Gloria Steinem and her 91 years of activism, The Shift is honoring 90 voices shaping our future. theshiftison grateful for the honour TheShiftIsOn.” The special edition titled The Shift 90 Plus One celebrates Gloria Steinem’s decades-long legacy and features a quote from Deepika: “Success to me isn't just about professional achievements but also about well-being where mental health and self-care matter as much as discipline, dedication, and determination. I believe in patience, balance, consistency, and authenticity, and I hope to inspire a generation that values these virtues.”

title: WAR 2 is India’s costliest spy film; Jr. NTR gets ₹70 Cr, Hrithik ₹50 Cr plus profits.
content: War 2, starring Hrithik Roshan and Jr. NTR, has become one of the most talked-about upcoming films in Indian cinema. Just a day after its trailer dropped, fans and industry insiders are buzzing over its grand visuals and international-level action. Slated for release on Independence Day weekend in 2025, the film is part of the YRF Spy Universe and promises a cinematic spectacle with six unique action sequences. According to trade sources, War 2 is YRF’s most expensive and ambitious project yet, made on a massive budget of ₹400 crore surpassing Tiger 3 (₹350 crore) and Pathaan (₹325 crore). This amount doesn’t even include the marketing and promotional costs. Jr. NTR has reportedly been paid ₹70 crore, while Hrithik Roshan is earning ₹50 crore upfront, along with a share of the profits. Kiara Advani has been paid ₹15 crore, and Anil Kapoor is taking home ₹10 crore. The total talent fees alone amount to ₹150 crore. Director Ayan Mukerji has also received ₹30 crore for helming the project. The remaining ₹220 crore has been used to create the film’s grand scale and next-level visuals. War 2 hits theatres on August 14, 2025, and expectations are sky-high. Ask ChatGPT

title: 17-year-old student in Spain suspected of creating nude images of classmates using AI.
content: Spanish police are investigating a 17-year-old student after several female classmates came forward with complaints about AI-generated nude images being circulated online. The probe began when 16 young women from the same educational institute reported that fake photos and videos showing them naked had appeared on social media, created using artificial intelligence. The first complaint was made in December by a girl who discovered an AI-generated video and photos of her, completely nude, posted on a social media account falsely using her name. As more students spoke up, police started suspecting that the images were created by someone within the school. They tracked the IP addresses linked to the fake accounts and traced them back to the home of the 17-year-old now under investigation. He is suspected of using AI technology to produce and sell these deepfake images, and he faces charges related to the corruption of minors. This case is not isolated; Spanish authorities have previously found instances of AI-generated pornographic images involving minors. In response, the Spanish government announced plans in March to introduce a law making it a crime to create and share non-consensual AI-generated sexual images. This proposed legislation, which would be a first in Europe, is still awaiting approval by parliament.

title: TCS plans to reduce its workforce by more than 12,000 employees in a significant job cut.
content: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), India’s largest IT services company, is set to cut around 12,200 jobs, which amounts to about 2% of its global workforce, mainly affecting middle and senior management. The company is undergoing a major restructuring driven by shifts in technology, especially the rise of AI, and changes in how business operates. While TCS is investing heavily in retraining and redeploying employees to adapt to new markets and technologies, some roles have become redundant, leading to these job cuts. TCS CEO K Krithivasan described the decision as one of the toughest of his career and emphasized that the company is trying to manage the transition carefully to avoid disrupting client services. He also mentioned delays in client projects and decision-making as contributing factors. Meanwhile, TCS has postponed the onboarding of over 600 experienced hires without prior notice, leaving many candidates in uncertainty. Although the company has assured that all job offers will be honoured, this move has caused anxiety and financial stress for many who resigned from previous jobs expecting to join TCS. The situation has drawn criticism from the Nascent Information Technology Employees Senate (NITES), which has appealed to the government for intervention, calling the company’s actions exploitative and unfair.

title: Could using higher ethanol blends lead to higher petrol prices?
content: India’s push to blend 20% ethanol with petrol aimed to reduce crude oil dependence and support farmers by using surplus sugar for ethanol production. Although ethanol costs less than petrol, this has not translated into lower fuel prices for consumers. Petrol prices across most states hover between ₹100 and ₹104 per litre, despite ethanol’s cheaper production cost. Fuel prices in India are influenced by global crude oil rates and taxes like excise duty and VAT, which have remained relatively stable except for a recent excise duty hike that hasn’t been passed on to buyers. A key reason for higher costs is ethanol’s lower energy content compared to petrol, meaning vehicles consume more fuel to travel the same distance, increasing running costs. Ethanol prices also vary based on their source, with some types costing more after transport and taxes are added. This suggests that blended petrol should cost around ₹95 per litre, a rate currently only seen in Delhi. While the ethanol blending program has helped cut crude oil imports and boost the economy, consumers haven’t benefited from lower prices. The government plans to increase ethanol blends to 27-30

title: PM Modi says Operation Sindoor shows terrorists have nowhere to hide in Tamil Nadu.
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi praised Operation Sindoor for showing the world how India firmly protects its sovereignty. Speaking at an event in Tamil Nadu commemorating the birth anniversary of the Chola emperor Rajendra Chola-I, Modi said the military strike sent a clear message that terrorists and enemies of India have no safe place to hide. The operation, carried out on May 7, 2025, targeted terrorist bases in Pakistan and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir in response to the deadly Pahalgam attack. Modi highlighted how the operation has sparked a renewed sense of confidence and pride across the country. He shared that during his visit to the event, people praised the operation enthusiastically, reflecting widespread public support. He emphasized that the world must recognize India’s growing strength. In addition to discussing the operation, Modi spoke about the historical significance of the Chola rulers, Raja Raja Chola and Rajendra Chola-I, whose names remain symbols of India’s identity and pride. He announced plans to build grand statues of both emperors in Tamil Nadu as “modern pillars” celebrating India’s rich heritage. Modi also pointed out India’s ancient democratic traditions, noting that the Chola-era Kudavolai system, used over a thousand years ago, preceded even the Magna Carta in promoting democratic governance.

title: Rohman Shawl’s ‘7th anniversary’ post sparks confusion about his relationship with Sushmita Sen.
content: Rohman Shawl recently posted a heartfelt message on Instagram, marking seven years since he first met Sushmita Sen. In his post, he reflected on the special bond they share one that goes beyond traditional labels. Rohman clarified that they are not a romantic couple but have a deep connection that has stood the test of time. He shared a black-and-white photo of the two of them together, with him holding Sushmita close. Along with the picture, he wrote a touching caption expressing how their relationship has evolved over the years. He reminisced about teaching her chess, only to be outplayed by her now, and how she helped him overcome his fear of water by teaching him to swim. Rohman also thanked her for giving him some of his best haircuts. Despite the closeness, Rohman made it clear that their relationship isn’t defined by traditional terms like “lovers,” but rather by the meaningful and unique bond they’ve built. His post left many wondering about the nature of their relationship, sparking curiosity and speculation among fans. However, Rohman’s note emphasizes that what they share is special and enduring, regardless of labels or expectations.

title: Mastermind of Pahalgam attack, Suleman Shah, killed in Kashmir encounter: Sources.
content: During a joint operation called Operation Mahadev, security forces killed three terrorists, including Suleman Shah, the mastermind behind the Pahalgam attack. The operation took place on Monday in the rugged Lidwas area near Dara, close to Dachigam National Park in Srinagar. The Indian Army and Jammu and Kashmir Police carried out the mission after receiving specific intelligence about the presence of foreign terrorists in the region. The encounter began around 11 a.m. in the dense forests between the Zabarwan and Mahadev ridges, which is how the operation got its name. The Army's Chinar Corps confirmed that three terrorists had been neutralized in a fierce firefight and that additional reinforcements, along with aerial surveillance, were deployed to support the ground forces. While sources have identified one of the deceased terrorists as Suleman Shah, police officials emphasized that the operation is still ongoing. They refrained from providing detailed information until the mission is fully complete. A police spokesperson said the exchange of fire continues in the higher reaches and that they would release more information when the situation is under control.

title: ‘Avatar: Fire and Ash’ trailer leaks online after exclusive screening, fans predict another billion for James Cameron.
content: The trailer for James Cameron’s upcoming film, Avatar: Fire and Ash, was exclusively shown in theaters alongside Fantastic Four: First Steps. However, shortly after the screening, the trailer was leaked online. According to The Hollywood Reporter, Disney had planned for the trailer to be seen only in cinemas, but unauthorized copies quickly spread across social media. Efforts are underway to remove the leaked trailer from the internet, with many versions already taken down. A similar situation happened recently with Christopher Nolan’s The Odyssey trailer, which was also released in theaters ahead of Jurassic Park Rebirth. Despite the removals, fan reactions to the Avatar trailer have been strong. One viewer described it as having “some of the most beautiful footage” they’d seen all year, highlighting intense scenes like fistfights inside volcanic eruptions and massive aerial battles. Another fan said the trailer shifted their feelings from “meh, more of the same” to “yup, another billion to James Cameron.” The trailer takes viewers back to Pandora, introducing two new tribes: the Wind Traders and the Ash People. It features familiar characters like Sam Worthington’s Jake Sully, Zoe Saldana’s Neytiri, and their Na’vi family caught up in a fierce conflict with the Ash People. The return of Colonel Miles Quaritch is also shown. At one point, Jake warns Neytiri, “We cannot live like this,” before being captured.

title: England wins Women’s Euro 2025 by beating Spain on penalties.
content: England triumphed over Spain in a tense penalty shootout to claim the Women’s Euro 2025 title on Sunday. After a 1-1 draw following extra time, Chloe Kelly confidently converted the winning penalty, securing a 3-1 shootout victory for the defending champions. England’s goalkeeper, Hannah Hampton, played a crucial role by saving penalties from Spain’s Mariona Caldentey and star player Aitana Bonmati. Meanwhile, Spain’s Salma Paralluelo missed her shot, sending it wide and ending their hopes. The match was full of drama, with England coming from behind after conceding an early goal to Spain’s Mariona Caldentey in the 25th minute. England fought back in the second half, with Alessia Russo heading in an equalizer from a Chloe Kelly cross in the 57th minute. Throughout the tournament, Spain had only been behind for four minutes total, and never trailed England for a single moment, but they were unable to clinch their first European championship. This victory was reminiscent of Kelly’s extra-time winner three years ago at Wembley when England defeated Germany 2-1, showing once again that England knows how to win in the most dramatic fashion.

title: Police Raid Pune Rave Party, Detain Husband of NCP Leader Rohini Khadse.
content: Late last night, Pune Police conducted a raid on a rave party held in a flat inside a lodge in the upscale Kharadi area. Seven people were detained, including Pranjal Khewalkar, who is the husband of Nationalist Congress Party (NCP) leader Rohini Khadse and son-in-law of senior politician Eknath Khadse. The party was reportedly disguised as a private house gathering. The police acted on a tip-off and found five men and two women at the location. During the search, narcotics, hookahs, and alcohol were seized. It was confirmed that the attendees were using drugs, smoking hookah, and consuming alcohol. Pranjal Khewalkar’s involvement has added political sensitivity to the case, given his connections to influential political families. All seven individuals have been booked under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act and other applicable laws. Authorities are continuing their investigation to uncover the sources of the drugs and the extent of the network behind these parties. Officials believe more names might come to light as the inquiry progresses. This incident has raised concerns about the prevalence of illegal and drug-fueled parties in Pune’s affluent neighborhoods, prompting questions about law enforcement, security measures, and political responsibility.

title: Three Suspects Wanted After Gang Rape in Lonavala.
content: Late Friday night in Lonavala, a young woman was allegedly abducted and gang-raped by three unknown men. The victim was walking near Tungarli village when a car approached her. The men inside threatened her, forced her into the vehicle, took away her phone, and tied her hands and legs. Over several hours, they reportedly raped her at different locations while driving around. The men eventually left her near Nangargaon, where she was found by locals in a frightened and confused state. They took her to a nearby temple for safety and called the police. The Lonavala Police arrived quickly, took her statement, and arranged for a medical examination. An FIR has been filed against the three unidentified men under sections of the Indian Penal Code including gang rape and wrongful confinement. Police have formed special teams to hunt for the suspects and are reviewing CCTV footage from the area. They are also questioning known criminals in the region to gather more information. The investigation is ongoing as authorities work to bring the perpetrators to justice.

title: Mahesh Bhatt shares Ranbir Kapoor’s thoughts on Alia Bhatt’s success.
content: Mahesh Bhatt recently praised his daughter Alia Bhatt for becoming a self-made star in Bollywood, highlighting her unique drive and willingness to take risks in her career. In a conversation with Himanshu Mehta, the filmmaker shared how Alia continues to impress him, both as an artist and as a person. He mentioned that he was initially surprised to discover her passion for acting, especially since even Karan Johar recognized her talent early on. Mahesh also spoke about the changes he’s noticed in Alia since she became a mother, saying she has developed a new depth and maturity. He described this phase as a different kind of coming-of-age for her. When asked about what Ranbir Kapoor thinks of Alia, Mahesh said Ranbir finds her ambition remarkable. “He’s laid-back and takes things easy, but Alia is a go-getter who always wants to do more,” Mahesh explained. Alia and Ranbir, who dated briefly before marrying in an intimate ceremony in 2022, recently welcomed their daughter, Raha. Alia’s upcoming projects include the action-packed film Alpha, part of the YRF Spy Universe, releasing on December 25, and Sanjay Leela Bhansali’s Love and War, which stars Ranbir Kapoor and Vicky Kaushal.

title: Indira Krishnan praises Ranbir Kapoor’s amazing dedication in transforming from Animal to Ramayana.
content: Indira Krishnan, who will portray Kaushalya in the upcoming mythological film Ramayana, recently shared her admiration for Ranbir Kapoor’s extraordinary transformation into Lord Ram. Having worked with him before in Animal, where she played his mother-in-law, she was amazed by how different his approach was this time. In an interview with Bollywood Bubble, she highlighted his intense dedication and meticulous preparation. Ranbir not only focused on his physical appearance but also worked hard on his eyes, posture, and overall presence to embody the divine character. She revealed that he would spend 3-4 hours daily working out, often arriving early on set by 7 a.m., and would discuss his workout routines after shooting. Indira emphasized that his commitment was “200 percent,” and the difference in his body language and demeanor was remarkable. She recalled being stunned when she first saw him in Lord Ram’s avatar during a scene involving a sandalwood ceremony. His calm, composed stance and the ease with which he carried himself showcased his dedication and discipline. Indira noted that the contrast between his gritty role in Animal and the divine persona in Ramayana was striking. She believes Ranbir’s transformation is a testament to his hard work and focus on delivering an authentic portrayal of Lord Ram in this epic film, directed by Nitesh Tiwari.

title: Gautam Gambhir criticized the pitch curator’s yelling, saying it annoyed the head coach.
content: During a training session at the Oval, Indian head coach Gautam Gambhir and the pitch curator, Lee Fortis, had a heated exchange. The trouble started when Fortis was upset with some players carrying drinks onto the field and shouted at them. Gambhir was unhappy with how Fortis spoke to his team and stepped in to defend them, saying, “You cannot talk to my players like that.” The situation quickly escalated, with Fortis threatening to file a complaint. However, the Indian team decided not to take any action, choosing instead to handle the matter quietly and professionally. Further details revealed that Gambhir’s reaction was a response to Fortis firing the first shots. Kotak, India’s batting coach, explained that Fortis was being overly strict by telling the team to stay 2.5 meters away from the pitch, which seemed unnecessary given it was a five-day Test match and the players were wearing joggers, not spikes. Kotak defended the team’s approach, stating that checking the pitch with rubber spikes is harmless and that the ground shows no damage. He emphasized that experienced players are well aware of the risks and know how to behave on the field. Despite the tension, India chose to rise above the incident, opting not to lodge any formal complaints. Kotak criticized Fortis’ attitude, reminding everyone that the team is composed of professionals who understand the importance of respecting the pitch and the game. The episode highlighted underlying tensions, but the team remained focused on the upcoming match, maintaining a calm and disciplined approach.

title: Soham Parekh comments on the TCS layoffs impacting the Indian IT sector, urging people not to wait.
content: After TCS announced it would cut around 2% of its global workforce, affecting over 12,000 employees, Soham Parekh shared his thoughts on the situation. The move surprised many in the Indian IT industry, with some criticizing it, while others saw it as inevitable given the rise of AI. Parekh, who had previously made headlines for being accused of working at multiple startups simultaneously an allegation he admitted to but said he did to support himself offered advice to tech workers. He suggested that they should work harder to stay ahead in the changing industry. Parekh responded to reports claiming TCS would freeze hiring for experienced staff and halt salary increases globally. He urged fellow tech employees not to wait for things to happen but to put in extra effort to outwork the system. Despite the controversy surrounding him, with some calling him a scammer and others praising his skills, Parekh maintained that he worked at multiple startups due to financial difficulties and was a dedicated developer. TCS clarified that the layoffs primarily target middle and senior management, and re-employment for these roles isn’t expected to be possible. The company said it has been working on reskilling and redeploying staff but, unfortunately, some employees will have to be let go because their roles can't be adapted to meet future needs.

title: Rahul Gandhi challenges PM Modi to call Trump a liar, saying, “Bol dega, sorry bol denge.
content: During a heated session in Parliament on Tuesday, Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge launched sharp attacks on Prime Minister Narendra Modi, demanding clarity on former US President Donald Trump’s repeated claims of mediating peace between India and Pakistan. Gandhi questioned why Modi was staying silent on the issue and challenged him to directly address Trump’s statements. He asked, “If Trump is lying about brokering the ceasefire, say it. Say it in Parliament,” emphasizing that the Prime Minister should show political courage if he believes Trump’s claims are false. Gandhi also criticized the government’s approach to Operation Sindoor, highlighting the need for strong political will and operational freedom when dealing with sensitive military matters.
