title: Jackie Chan thinks Hollywood movies aren’t as good these days because big studios are run by businesspeople, not filmmakers.
content: Jackie Chan, the global film icon and martial arts superstar, recently received the Career Leopard award at the 78th Locarno Film Festival. During the event, he shared his thoughts on the current state of Hollywood, expressing concern that big studios prioritize business over creativity. He explained that many studios are run by people who focus more on making money than on filmmaking, which makes it harder to produce good movies today. In a conversation with Locarno festival head Giona Nazzaro, Jackie reflected on his long and varied career, from his early days as a background performer in films like Fist of Fury to becoming a leading star in the 70s and 80s. He recounted stories about his dangerous stunts, many of which he performed himself, and talked about his transition to Hollywood, where he found success with movies like Rumble in the Bronx, Rush Hour, and Shanghai Noon. Jackie Chan is widely regarded as one of the most influential martial artists in cinema history. Over the years, his films have earned nearly $6 billion worldwide. Despite the challenges he sees in today’s industry, his impact on action and martial arts films remains unmatched, and he continues to be a beloved figure around the globe.

title: ‘Won’t give in to nuclear blackmail’: India on Pak army chief Asim Munir’s threats.
content: Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir recently made provocative remarks during a gathering with the Pakistani diaspora in Florida, asserting that Pakistan could use its nuclear weapons to take down India and “half the world” if faced with an existential threat. He also threatened to destroy Indian dams following the suspension of the Indus Waters Treaty. These statements have raised serious concerns about Pakistan’s nuclear command and control, especially given the military’s close connections with terrorist groups. India’s External Affairs Ministry condemned Munir’s remarks as irresponsible and regrettable, particularly because they were made on US soil—a country considered friendly to Pakistan. Spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal emphasized that India will not give in to nuclear intimidation and remains committed to safeguarding its national security. The ministry pointed out that such nuclear sabre-rattling is a familiar tactic from Pakistan and deepens doubts about the country’s role as a responsible nuclear power. Experts and officials familiar with the issue highlighted that Munir’s threats reflect Pakistan’s history of nuclear brinkmanship, which fuels instability in South Asia. There is also widespread international concern that Pakistan’s nuclear materials or expertise could fall into the hands of terrorist groups, given the lack of transparency and accountability within the military. These factors contribute to the global community’s wariness toward Pakistan’s nuclear arsenal and the risks it poses to regional and international security.

title: Censor Board Cuts 9 Seconds from Kiara Advani's Bikini Scene in War 2, Requests Less Sensual Content.
content: War 2, starring Hrithik Roshan, Kiara Advani, and Jr NTR, faced some last-minute edits before its release on the eve of Independence Day. One of the notable changes was a 9-second cut from Kiara Advani’s bikini scene in the song "Aava Jaava," following a directive from the Central Board of Certification (CBFC) to reduce sensual content by half, according to Bollywood Hungama. While the CBFC didn’t specifically mention the bikini scene, it was reportedly trimmed to tone down the visuals. An industry insider revealed that the film was cleared by the Revising Committee (RC), not the Examining Committee (EC), which likely requested several changes. The RC, chaired by Padma Shri Ramesh Patange, is known for approving films after the EC raises concerns, often with some modifications. This isn’t the first time the RC has stepped in to clear films that initially faced objections. War 2 is the sequel to the 2019 hit War and marks the first collaboration between Hrithik Roshan and Jr NTR. Directed by Ayan Mukerji, known for his romantic films, the movie promises a mix of high-octane action and philosophical moments. Hrithik shared a heartfelt post on Instagram after wrapping up the 149-day shoot, expressing pride in the hard work put into the project. The film is set to hit theaters on August 14.

title: Army stops major infiltration by Pakistan's 'BAT' near LoC in Uri, soldier martyred.
content: The Indian Army successfully stopped an infiltration attempt along the Line of Control (LoC) in the Uri sector of Jammu and Kashmir’s Baramulla district, officials reported on Wednesday. The operation involved a clash near Tikka Post, with militants believed to be supported by Pakistan’s Border Action Team (BAT). Unfortunately, one Indian soldier was killed during the exchange of fire, although the army has yet to officially confirm the casualty. This incident follows the recent death of Sepoy Banoth Anil Kumar, who lost his life while on duty along the LoC in Baramulla. The Indian Army’s Chinar Corps paid tribute to his bravery and expressed condolences to his family, honoring his sacrifice in service to the nation. Meanwhile, fighting continues along the border as security forces remain vigilant against infiltration attempts. Just days earlier, Chief of Defence Staff General Anil Chauhan and Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi mourned the deaths of two soldiers, Lance Naik Pritpal Singh and Sepoy Harminder Singh, during anti-terror operations in South Kashmir’s Kulgam district. The Integrated Defence Staff extended their deepest condolences to the families, saluting the soldiers’ ultimate sacrifice and reaffirming their support during these difficult times.

title: CJI to Review Delhi Stray Dogs Case Amid Concerns Over Humane and Legal Approach.
content: New Delhi: Chief Justice of India (CJI) has decided to examine the Delhi stray dogs case, raising hopes for a more humane and legally balanced resolution. This development could impact the August 11 order, which was widely criticised for being one-sided against the stray dog population. The move follows a lawyer’s submission citing an earlier Supreme Court ruling from May 2024 by Justice Maheshwari, which declared that harassing any living being is unconstitutional. The ruling also prohibited killing animals without due process and mandated proper facilities for relocation. The contradiction between the May judgment and the August order has opened the door for the case to be reconsidered. Possibility of Case ReopeningWith this legal conflict highlighted, there is now a strong possibility that the matter will be relisted for consideration. Delhi residents and animal rights activists, who have been protesting against the August order, see this as a chance for justice for both people and animals. Concerns Over Judicial BiasThe situation has also sparked debate over judicial impartiality. Observers point out that two judges have delivered starkly opposite decisions within months, raising questions about potential unconscious bias. Critics argue that personal perspectives—such as a judge’s own experience with pets—can sometimes influence rulings, despite the expectation of complete neutrality. Call for Humane, Practical SolutionsActivists emphasise that stray dog management must follow due process, as seen in many other countries. Relocation centres must be adequately equipped before shifting large numbers of animals, ensuring both public safety and animal welfare. With the CJI’s intervention, the coming weeks could prove decisive for the future of stray dog policy in Delhi.

title: Rajinikanth at 50 years: How he said he might have been an underworld don if not an actor.
content: Did you know that there was a time when Rajinikanth thought he might have become an underworld don if he hadn’t pursued acting? The Tamil superstar, now 74, is about to celebrate 50 years since his debut in the 1975 film Apoorva Raagangal by K Balachander. Before rising to fame, he worked as a coolie and a bus conductor, facing many struggles along the way. In a 2010 interview with The Telegraph India, Rajinikanth shared that if acting hadn’t worked out, he might have turned to smuggling or even become a don to make money. He reflected on this phase, saying, “Yes, that’s true, I once thought about becoming an underworld don. But that time is behind me now I’ve earned enough.” This is particularly interesting since, although he never became a don in real life, Rajinikanth has famously played don characters on screen in films like Billa (1980), Baashha (1985), and Kabali (2016). Starting his career mostly in villain and supporting roles, Rajinikanth has remained a beloved hero for five decades. Many celebrities, including Kamal Haasan, Hrithik Roshan, Mammootty, and Mohanlal, have congratulated him on this milestone. His latest movie, Coolie, directed by Lokesh Kanagaraj, released on 14 August and smashed several box office records on its opening day.

title: Hours after Putin’s talks hit a snag, Trump says again he prevented a nuclear war between India and Pakistan.
content: US President Donald Trump once again claimed that he played a key role in preventing a war between India and Pakistan, saying his efforts were driven by a desire to save lives and stop conflicts from escalating. Speaking on Fox News, Trump defended his involvement in global disputes, including those far from the US, such as the recent ceasefire between Thailand and Cambodia. He insisted that without his pressure, wars would have broken out or worsened, highlighting his administration’s push for peace as part of his foreign policy achievements. Trump specifically pointed to the India-Pakistan situation, saying that tensions had escalated to the point of planes being shot down and a nuclear conflict was close. However, India has repeatedly denied Trump's claims, with External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar clarifying that the ceasefire was reached through direct talks between military officials and that there was no US involvement or trade-related discussions tied to the agreement. Despite this, Trump continues to assert that his role was crucial in calming the situation. These statements came shortly after a summit between Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin failed to yield any progress on the war in Ukraine. Trump also claimed he pressured Putin to end the conflict within a set deadline. Throughout recent months, Trump has frequently highlighted ceasefires and diplomatic efforts, portraying them as successes of his administration’s foreign policy, even as some details have been disputed by the countries involved.

title: Senior Indian player lost his temper and grabbed Irfan Pathan’s jersey after the all-rounder was sent to bat at number three.
content: Former India all-rounder Irfan Pathan recently shared a lesser-known story from his playing days, revealing a tense moment in the dressing room over his batting position. Known for his skill as a left-arm pacer and handy batsman, Irfan enjoyed a successful career but admitted that not everything was smooth behind the scenes. He recalled an incident, possibly during a series against Sri Lanka or Pakistan, when a senior teammate got upset because Irfan was sent to bat at number three, a spot the senior player believed he deserved. Irfan explained that the senior player thought he was a better batsman and took offense at being placed below Irfan in the batting order. The situation escalated to the point where the player physically grabbed Irfan’s jersey in frustration. As a young player, Irfan chose not to respond and didn’t name the senior player out of respect and to avoid unnecessary conflict, noting that friendships and rivalries in cricket are never permanent. When asked if the incident involved any of India’s cricket legends like Ganguly, Tendulkar, Dravid, Sehwag, or Laxman, Irfan quickly clarified that it wasn’t any of them. The story highlighted the pressure and emotions that can surface in a competitive team environment, especially when young players are given responsibility in key positions.

title: Coolie Worldwide Box Office Day 2: Rajinikanth’s Film Earns Over ₹250 Crore, Becomes 2025’s Highest-Grossing Tamil Movie.
content: Rajinikanth’s latest film, Coolie, has made a massive impact at the box office, crossing the ₹250 crore mark worldwide just two days after its release. Taking advantage of the Independence Day holiday, the movie saw strong ticket sales both in India and overseas. In the first two days, Coolie earned ₹118.50 crore net (₹145 crore gross) in India and raked in over $13 million (around ₹110 crore) from international markets, making it the highest-grossing Tamil film of 2025 so far. The film’s success is even more impressive given that most South Indian star-driven films see a sharp drop after the opening day, but Coolie managed to keep that decline to just 17%, thanks to great word of mouth. Coolie’s performance has also reignited discussions about Rajinikanth’s place in Tamil cinema. Despite years of debate that Ajith Kumar or Vijay might have overtaken him as the top star, Coolie’s numbers prove otherwise. In just two days, the film has surpassed the lifetime collections of Ajith’s releases this year, including Vidaamuyarchi (₹138 crore) and Good Bad Ugly (₹248 crore). It has also beaten other popular Tamil films like Dragon (₹150 crore), solidifying Rajinikanth’s standing as the reigning superstar in the industry. Directed by Lokesh Kanagaraj, Coolie stars Rajinikanth in the lead role and features a strong supporting cast including Nagarjuna, Shruti Haasan, Soubin Shahir, and Upendra, with a cameo by Aamir Khan. If the film continues its current momentum through the weekend, experts believe it could cross ₹400 crore in just its opening weekend, putting it on track to challenge the all-time Tamil box office record of ₹691 crore set by Rajinikanth’s 2.0.

title: PAL Foundation Holds Candle March for the Voiceless in Andheri.
content: https://www.youtube.com/shorts/-Rdbn4D5cLg Andheri, 17th August 2025 — In a remarkable display of compassion and unity, the PAL Foundation successfully organized a Candle March for the Voiceless at Andheri. The event brought together animal lovers, feeders, rescuers, and concerned citizens, all united by a common mission — to raise awareness about animal suffering and to give a voice to those who cannot speak for themselves. Despite the ongoing monsoon season, nature itself seemed to stand in solidarity, as not a single drop of rain fell until the rally concluded. Participants walked peacefully with glowing candles and heartfelt placards, creating a powerful atmosphere of hope, empathy, and justice. What made the evening even more impactful was the unexpected support from bystanders. Many who had no direct connection to animal welfare paused to watch, listen, and ultimately stand with the marchers. Several voiced their agreement, saying, “Whatever is happening is wrong.” This spontaneous outpouring of solidarity showed that the movement’s message resonated deeply with people from all walks of life. It proved that compassion for animals is not limited to those already involved in rescue or feeding work — it is a universal human value that can inspire anyone when they witness suffering first-hand. “Stray dogs may not have homes, but they teach us the truest meaning of loyalty, love, and survival.” This thought, echoed during the march, served as a reminder of the resilience and purity animals bring into human lives. The streets of Andheri were illuminated not just by candlelight but also by the spirit of unity and kindness that participants radiated. The Candle March for the Voiceless was not just an event but a movement — a reminder that even small acts of solidarity can spark big changes. Citizens from varied backgrounds stood shoulder to shoulder, raising their voices against cruelty and for compassion. The evening carried a strong message: when humanity unites for the voiceless, change is not only possible but inevitable. The march symbolized the beginning of a stronger collective movement in Mumbai to protect and respect stray animals, inspiring many more to step forward and join the cause. A spokesperson from PAL Foundation expressed heartfelt gratitude, saying, “We thank all the compassionate souls who came forward. Together, we proved that when humanity speaks up, change is not just a dream — it becomes possible.” The Foundation also extended a special thanks to the Senior Police Inspector, Mumbai Police, and to every animal lover, feeder, and rescuer who supported the march. Their courage and dedication continue to inspire society to treat stray dogs and all animals with dignity and care. As the rally concluded, participants left with renewed hope and determination — a belief that compassion is humanity’s strongest force, and when united, it can light the path toward justice for every living being.

title: Over 100 crew members fall ill in Leh, Ranveer Singh’s Dhurandhar shoot put on hold: Report.
content: During the shoot of Ranveer Singh’s upcoming film Dhurandhar in Ladakh, a major health scare forced the team to halt work after more than 120 crew members fell ill, reportedly due to food poisoning. The incident took place on August 17 at Pathar Sahib in Leh, where a large portion of the unit had been filming. According to officials quoted by Mid-Day, over a hundred crew members had to be admitted to Sajal Narbbu Memorial Hospital after they complained of severe stomach pain, vomiting, and headaches shortly after their meal. Out of the nearly 600 people who were served food that day, around 120 required urgent medical attention. There were also unconfirmed reports that a few workers experienced heart-related complications, which added to the concern. Health authorities have since collected food samples for testing to determine what caused the contamination. Until then, the production has temporarily suspended shooting. Dhurandhar is Ranveer Singh’s first film with director Aditya Dhar, known for Uri: The Surgical Strike. The movie promises an intense narrative of betrayal, courage, and hidden battles fought in secrecy. The cast includes Sanjay Dutt, Akshaye Khanna, R. Madhavan, and Arjun Rampal. The film is set to release worldwide on December 5, 2025.

title: Vivek Agnihotri threatens legal action if The Bengal Files is stopped from releasing in West Bengal.
content: Filmmaker Vivek Ranjan Agnihotri has warned that he will take legal action if his upcoming film The Bengal Files is prevented from releasing in West Bengal. His statement came after the film’s trailer launch in Kolkata faced multiple disruptions. First, a multiplex cancelled the event, then power went out at the hotel where it was shifted, and finally, the police arrived to question whether the organizers had the required permissions. The film, which is set to release on September 5, focuses on the Calcutta riots of August 16, 1946, sparked by the Muslim League’s call for “Direct Action Day.” Addressing the controversy around Gopal Chandra Mukherjee whose grandson has filed a case alleging the film wrongly portrays his grandfather Agnihotri clarified that the character shown is only “inspired” and not the central figure in the story. He stressed that his intention was not to distort history, adding that the film shows only what Mukherjee himself said in an old BBC interview. “I have nothing to do with his personal life or politics. I have shown him as a hero,” the director explained, while also hinting that political pressure may be behind the objections raised. Agnihotri described The Bengal Files as part of his effort to bring forward “untold stories of India.” He said his work is focused on Hindu civilization and history, and that he does not attempt to make films on Islamic or Christian history. Framing the controversy as an attempt to suppress free expression, he added that if the West Bengal government tries to block the release, he will challenge it legally, hoping that “sanity prevails” and the film reaches audiences as planned.

title: PM Modi will launch new Kolkata Metro lines on August 22 – check routes and fares.
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit West Bengal on Friday, August 22, to inaugurate several important projects, including new sections of the Kolkata Metro. Around 4:15 pm, he will flag off metro services on the newly built routes and also take a ride from Jessore Road Metro Station to Jai Hind Bimanbandar before returning. Along with this, the Prime Minister will also travel to Bihar the same day to launch various developmental projects and lay foundation stones for upcoming works. The newly constructed metro network in Kolkata covers a stretch of 13.61 km. It includes three major routes — Noapara to Jai Hind Bimanbandar, Sealdah to Esplanade, and Beleghata to Hemanta Mukhopadhyay. To mark the inauguration, PM Modi will personally flag off the Noapara–Jai Hind Bimanbandar service from Jessore Road. The Sealdah–Esplanade and Beleghata–Hemanta Mukhopadhyay services will be launched simultaneously through video conferencing. In addition to these metro services, the Prime Minister will also inaugurate a newly built subway at Howrah Metro Station. These projects are expected to improve connectivity across Kolkata and reduce traffic pressure on roads, offering commuters a faster and more convenient travel option.

title: Old fossil fuel plants are now being turned into green energy hubs.
content: For many years, the Tamaya power station in Chile’s Atacama Desert supplied electricity to the local region by running on diesel fuel. It was once a typical example of how fossil fuels powered communities, despite their heavy environmental cost. Now, that same site has been transformed. Instead of a noisy, polluting generator, rows of gleaming solar panels capture the desert sun, supported by advanced battery storage technology. This shift marks a new chapter for the region, showing how clean energy can replace outdated and harmful systems. The transformation was carried out by Engie, a French energy company, earlier this year. According to Juan Villavicencio, the firm’s head in Chile, the Tamaya site now represents a powerful symbol—where the traditional fossil-fuel past meets the sustainable energy future. It stands as proof that energy infrastructure can evolve to be both modern and environmentally friendly.

title: India Bans Real-Money Games, Causing ₹23,000 Crore Industry to Disappear Overnight.
content: India has passed a new law banning real-money online games, causing major gaming companies to suspend operations and leading to layoffs. The Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Bill, 2025, now awaits the President’s approval. Leading firms like Dream Sports (owner of Dream11), Mobile Premier League (MPL), and Zupee have already started halting their real-money gaming services. Fantasy sports apps such as Dream Picks and Dream Play have stopped functioning, while Dream11 and similar cash-based games remain live temporarily until the ban is enforced. Dream Sports confirmed it will shut down all real-money games once the law takes effect. This ban impacts a $23 billion market with revenues around ₹31,000 crore and tax contributions near ₹20,000 crore. MPL has stopped accepting deposits and will allow withdrawals from August 22, while Zupee continues offering free games but has suspended paid ones. The law aims to tackle gambling addiction, financial losses, cheating, and underage gaming, imposing strict penalties including up to three years in prison and fines up to ₹1 crore. It also creates a regulatory body for esports and social gaming but bans real-money formats. This move marks a major change in India’s gaming landscape, creating uncertainty for employees, businesses, and fans of fantasy and real-money games.

title: Manish Malhotra to unveil the first look of Gustaakh Ishq on Monday; film scheduled for release in November.
content: Fashion designer Manish Malhotra will reveal the first look of his film, Gustaakh Ishq, on Monday. This marks his debut as a film producer under Stage 5 Production. He shared the news on social media and confirmed that the movie is set to release in November. In his message, Manish said, “Since I was a child, I’ve had a deep love for cinema—the magic of stories, the glow of the big screen, and the emotions that stay with you long after the movie ends.” He added, “That love has shaped who I am, and now I’m overjoyed to share a dream come true. This November, 2025, my first film as a producer, Gustaakh Ishq - Kuch Pehle Jaisa, will hit theaters. It’s a film born out of passion, poetry, and a celebration of love in all its forms.” He also mentioned, “This Monday, I’ll be sharing the very first glimpse into the poetic world of Gustaakh Ishq. This journey is special, personal, and truly close to my heart. Movies have always been my passion since childhood.” With Gustaakh Ishq, Manish is moving from fashion and couture to storytelling through film. His debut production combines poetry and love, and fans are excited to see what the movie brings.

title: Harbhajan Singh accuses Lalit Modi of leaking slapgate video for his own selfish gain.
content: Harbhajan Singh has finally broken his silence on the release of the infamous 2008 slapgate video, which was made public by former IPL chairman Lalit Modi. The video, revealed during Modi’s chat on Michael Clarke’s podcast, shows Harbhajan slapping Sreesanth after a match between Mumbai Indians and Kings XI Punjab, sparking fresh discussions about the long-forgotten incident. Reacting to the leak, Harbhajan expressed disappointment, calling it unnecessary and motivated. He told Instant Bollywood, “The way the video has been leaked, it’s wrong. Something that happened years ago, people had moved on, but they are reminding everyone about it. I feel there’s a selfish motive behind this.” He also admitted feeling ashamed about the incident, saying mistakes happen in the heat of the game. The original footage was never aired back in 2008, as broadcasters cut to commercials during the altercation. Now, with the video resurfacing, Harbhajan insists it should have been left in the past. What’s your take should old controversies stay buried.

title: “SCO Summit 2025: PM Modi Exposes Pakistan on Global Stage.
content: https://youtu.be/35Z7dnRLvAY

title: “Breaking Through Hidden Hunger: India’s Next Big Health Victory.
content: India’s growth dream needs a healthy population. Yet hidden hunger, caused by lack of vital vitamins and minerals, silently weakens millions. Food fortification—adding nutrients to staples like rice, wheat, oil, milk, and salt—is a proven, safe, and low-cost way to fight malnutrition. India must act boldly: expand fortification, add missing nutrients, spread awareness, and make it a national mission for a healthier future.

title: “Google Pulls 77 Harmful Apps from Play Store Over Security Risks.
content: Google has pulled 77 malicious apps from the Play Store, part of a massive cleanup that saw nearly 4 million apps removed in the past year for violations. Most removals were tied to privacy and data issues. By early 2024, the Play Store had already lost nearly half its apps, with 155,000 developer accounts cut. Deleted apps remain on devices but can’t get updates. Google urges users to uninstall outdated apps, as Play Protect may flag dangerous ones for removal. Experts warn this “digital purge” may give a false sense of safety. A new Uninstall button is being tested to simplify deletions and protect users from threats.

title: Kriti Sanon slams Bollywood’s gender bias: says male stars get bigger perks while women are sidelined.
content: Kriti Sanon has spoken out about the gender bias that continues to exist in Bollywood, pointing to the subtle but significant ways women are often treated differently from their male co-stars. In a candid conversation, she shared that male actors are usually given better cars or bigger rooms than female actors. While she clarified that it’s not about luxury, Kriti said the inequality makes her feel smaller simply because she is a woman. The actress also highlighted how assistant directors (ADs) often call female actors to the set earlier, while waiting for their male counterparts to arrive later. She revealed that she has personally had to ask ADs not to follow such practices, as they reflect a deeper mindset problem in the industry. Kriti credited her upbringing for shaping her belief in equality. She recalled how her parents divided responsibilities at home and encouraged her and her sister Nupur to dream freely without restrictions. She added that her mother sacrificed her hobbies just to secure her right to study, a lesson that continues to inspire her. Currently, Kriti is enjoying the success of her recent project Do Patti and will next be seen in Tere Ishk Mein opposite Dhanush, releasing this November.

title: Breaking: PM takes swipe at Trump’s tariffs, says India is thriving despite global economic selfishness.
content: PM Modi indirectly slammed Trump tariffs, saying India’s economy grew 7.8% despite global “economic selfishness.” He urged trust in India’s future growth. US President Donald Trump imposed 50% tariffs on Indian goods to push his “Make America Great Again” agenda. In response, PM Narendra Modi, speaking at the Semicon India 2025 event in Delhi on Tuesday, indirectly hit back at the move. He highlighted that India’s economy grew 7.8% in April–June, its highest in five quarters, despite global challenges caused by “economic selfishness.” Modi emphasized that the world trusts India and is ready to collaborate on semiconductors. India has called the US tariffs “unjustified,” even as Modi projected confidence in India’s economic strength and resilience.

title: Exclusive: Apple demands suppliers shift to robots for iPhones, Macs and Watches
content: Apple is reportedly pushing its suppliers to adopt robotics in manufacturing, covering iPhones, iPads, Macs, and Watches. According to a DigiTimes report, the shift began in 2025 as part of Apple’s strategy to cut reliance on human labor, stabilize quality, and reduce long-term costs. Unlike earlier practices, suppliers must now handle automation upgrades themselves, raising upfront costs and causing temporary disruptions. Apple believes robotics will not only standardize production across global factories but also support its 2030 carbon neutrality goals while reducing risks linked to labor shortages and geopolitical challenges. Apple’s Q1 GDP growth (India retail) mirrors global expansion. 7.8% YoY growth reported in April–June quarter for India’s economy. Apple aims for 100% carbon neutrality by 2030. “Apple wants suppliers to handle automation independently,” DigiTimes report. “Robotics will reduce risks tied to labor shortages,”industry sources.

title: Hardik Pandya shows off new look before Asia Cup, fans compare him to Nicholas Pooran.
content: Hardik Pandya has unveiled a fresh new hairstyle just before the Asia Cup 2025, grabbing the attention of fans and cricket lovers. The Mumbai Indians skipper landed in Dubai on Thursday and will begin training for the much-awaited eight-team tournament on Friday. Looking stylish with a sandy blonde short haircut, Hardik shared his makeover on Instagram with the caption “New me.” Social media has since been buzzing, with fans comparing his new look to cricketers like Ben Stokes and Nicholas Pooran. Known as one of the best white-ball all-rounders, Hardik is once again in the spotlight off the fieldHardik Pandya’s short blonde haircut ahead of Asia Cup 2025 has gone viral, with fans drawing comparisons to Nicholas Pooran and Ben Stokes. Hardik Pandya’s blonde short haircut ahead of Asia Cup 2025 has gone viral, with fans drawing comparisons to Nicholas Pooran and Ben Stokes.

title: Trump claims India and Russia are slipping into China’s grip in major statement.
content: US President Donald Trump on Friday claimed that India and Russia appear to be “lost” to China, following the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) summit in Tianjin this week. Sharing a photo of PM Modi, Xi Jinping, and Vladimir Putin together, Trump wrote on Truth Social: “Looks like we’ve lost India and Russia to deepest, darkest, China. May they have a long and prosperous future together!” His comment comes after the summit, which highlighted growing ties between India, China, and Russia, and at a time when US-India relations are strained due to trade disputes and rising tensions over tariffs. Trump’s remark followed the SCO summit, where Modi, Xi, and Putin displayed strong camaraderie, attracting global attention. The US-India relationship has worsened after Trump doubled tariffs on Indian goods, creating tension in bilateral trade. Trump criticized India for a “one-sided” trade relationship, selling little to India while importing massive amounts of Indian goods. Former US officials say Trump’s trade policies pushed India closer to China, damaging decades of personal rapport with Modi.

title: "Indian Oil Leads Bold Push to Fully Revive Russian Crude Imports Against US Tariffs.
content: Indian Oil and peers seek full comeback of Russian crude, yet October offers are thin as cargoes shift to China. The US raised tariffs on Russia imports to 50%, but India resists, saying oil needs outweigh Washington’s pressure. OPEC+ eased supply limits, opening more Middle East exports, adding competition even as Moscow redirects barrels. India vows to keep buying from Russia, though flows dropped sharply in August, signaling peak imports are past.

title: "Sanjay Dutt Reveals Terrifying Jail Ordeal with Double-Murder Convict Who Held a Razor to His Neck.
content: Sanjay Dutt recently opened up about one of the scariest moments of his prison life while appearing on The Great Indian Kapil Show with his close friend Suniel Shetty. The 66-year-old actor revisited a dark chapter from his years behind bars following his conviction under the Arms Act related to the 1993 Mumbai blasts. He recalled a chilling encounter when jail officials asked him to shave his overgrown beard. Instead of assigning a barber, they handed the task to another inmate—a man serving time for double murder—who was given a razor and asked to shave him. As the inmate, Mishra, held a razor to his neck, Sanjay casually asked about his sentence. When Mishra replied “double murder,” Sanjay froze and quickly stopped his hand, realizing the danger. Though the incident left him shaken, Sanjay shared that he has no regrets in life except losing his parents early. He deeply misses his father, Sunil Dutt, and his mother, Nargis, who passed before his debut. Convicted for illegal possession of arms, Sanjay served five years at Yerwada Jail. His sentence was shortened for good behavior, during which he even hosted a prison radio show to inspire fellow inmates. On the film front, Sanjay stars in Baaghi 4 with Tiger Shroff and others. He will next be seen in The Raja Saab with Prabhas in October, followed by Dhurandar with Ranveer Singh and R. Madhavan in December.

title: “CM Yogi Orders Stern Action Against Bullies, Vows to Free Poor’s Land in Gorakhpur”
content: In Gorakhpur, Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath once again showed his firm stance against injustice. At the Janata Darshan held in the Gorakhnath temple, a distressed woman stepped forward and complained that local bullies had illegally occupied her land. Without wasting a moment, the CM directed police and administrative officials to act immediately, free the land, and ensure that such incidents are never repeated. His sharp response reassured the gathering that no one would be allowed to exploit the poor under his watch. Later, while addressing people at the Mahant Digvijaynath Smriti Auditorium, CM Yogi met around 200 individuals and patiently listened to their grievances. He made it clear that every application must be resolved fairly, quickly, and with justice for the victims. Many people also appealed for financial help for medical treatment. The CM assured them of full government support, instructing officials to prepare cost estimates quickly and send them for approval. He further stressed that all police and revenue cases should be handled with transparency and sensitivity. Meeting children at the Janata Darshan, he encouraged them to study sincerely, handed out chocolates, and reminded parents that education, books, and other facilities are available free of cost

title: “Charlie Kirk’s Killer Still at Large: FBI Frees George Zinn and Zachariah Qureshi as Nationwide Manhunt Escalates.
content: Charlie Kirk (Victim): 31-year-old conservative activist, co-founder of Turning Point USA, and close ally of Donald Trump. Fatally shot at a Utah Valley University student event. George Zinn & Zachariah Qureshi (Released suspects): Initially arrested after the shooting but quickly released after questioning when officers found no connection to the crime. Shooter (Unidentified, keyword: “person of interest”): Still at large. Described as wearing dark clothing; believed to have carried out a targeted assassination. Law Enforcement (FBI, Utah DPS, local police): Leading a massive manhunt with thousands of tips and community involvement. Conservative activist Charlie Kirk was fatally shot Wednesday in what officials described as a targeted assassination during a student event at Utah Valley University. The 31-year-old, co-founder of Turning Point USA and ally of President Donald Trump, was struck by a single bullet in front of roughly 3,000 attendees. Authorities said the motive remains unclear, but investigators are now pursuing a new person of interest after two men initially detained were cleared of involvement. Time of shooting: 12:20 p.m. MST Location: Utah Valley University outdoor student event Attendance: ~3,000 students & supporters Victim: Charlie Kirk, pronounced dead hours later at Timpanogos Regional Hospital No one remains in custody as of Wednesday evening,” said Utah DPS. FBI confirmed George Zinn and Zachariah Qureshi were released after questioning. Authorities are showing locals a photo of a person of interest dressed in dark clothing.

title: Nepal President Sends Strong Message as Gen Z Pushes for Interim PM.
content: Nepal is witnessing intense discussions as Gen-Z representatives, President Ram Chandra Paudel, and army chief Ashok Raj Sigdel come together to address the leadership vacuum in the country. In a letter dated September 11, President Paudel assured that he is making every possible effort to resolve the ongoing crisis within the constitutional framework and safeguard democracy. The President and the army have urged Gen-Z leaders to finalize a candidate for interim Prime Minister, stressing that the responsibility lies with them. Crowds of young citizens gathered outside army headquarters, waiting for updates as the talks continued on Thursday. Wednesday’s meeting ended without consensus, though names like former chief justice Sushila Karki and ex-Nepal power board chief Kulman Ghising were discussed. Kathmandu mayor Balendra Shah is also being considered as a possible interim PM candidate. President Paudel’s letter appealed to all sides to trust that a solution will emerge soon to address citizens’ demands. The army spokesperson confirmed that talks are centered on breaking the deadlock while maintaining law and order. The interim PM will replace KP Sharma Oli, who stepped down on Tuesday after violent student-led protests. In parts of Kathmandu, small groups of students staged demonstrations, calling for the Constitution to be upheld and democracy protected during the transition.

title: Aishwarya Rai Bachchan Secures Major Win in Delhi High Court as Her Personality Rights Are Protected.
content: In a landmark order, the Delhi High Court has granted interim relief to Bollywood star Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, barring anyone from using her name, image, likeness, or other personal traits without her permission. Justice Tejas Karia, while issuing the injunction, said that unauthorized use of her identity not only breaches her personality rights but also infringes on her privacy and dignity. The ruling makes it clear that misuse through Artificial Intelligence, face morphing, deepfakes, or other digital tools will not be tolerated. Google has been asked to take down flagged links within 72 hours, while the IT Ministry has been told to block all such URLs within a week. The Court explained that personality rights give every individual the power to control how their image, name, or likeness is used, including any commercial benefits that flow from them. Unauthorized exploitation, it said, both strips a person of control over their identity and undermines their right to live with dignity. Justice Karia observed that using Aishwarya Rai Bachchan’s persona without consent could mislead people into believing she had endorsed such content, thereby amounting to infringement. The case will now be taken up for further proceedings before the Joint Registrar on November 7, 2025, with a full hearing scheduled for January 15, 2026.

title: Pakistan threatens ICC: will boycott Asia Cup 2025 if PCB’s demands are ignored, pullout could be imminent.
content: The Pakistan Cricket Board (PCB) has issued a strong warning to the International Cricket Council (ICC), stating that they could pull out of the 2025 Asia Cup if their concerns are ignored. The dispute stems from the post-match controversy between India and Pakistan in Dubai on Sunday, where players from both sides did not shake hands. The issue intensified on Monday when PCB escalated its protest, not only against India but also targeting the match referee Andy Pycroft. The board has demanded his immediate removal from the tournament’s referee panel, accusing him of siding with one team and violating the spirit of cricket. PCB chairman Mohsin Naqvi, who also heads the Asian Cricket Council (ACC), announced on social media that a formal complaint had been lodged with the ICC. According to PCB, Pycroft overstepped his role by instructing the two captains — India’s Suryakumar Yadav and Pakistan’s Salam Agha not to shake hands at the toss, which the board believes was a clear breach of the ICC Code of Conduct and the MCC Laws. The demand for Pycroft’s removal has been positioned as non-negotiable, with reports suggesting PCB could even boycott their next Asia Cup fixture against the UAE on September 17 if action is not taken. While Cricbuzz noted the information has not been independently verified, Pakistani outlets such as CricketPakistan.com have reported that the board is firm on its stance. The controversy comes amid speculation that the ACC is also weighing possible disciplinary measures against India for their role in the handshake standoff.

title: Ukraine plans to slash Indian diesel imports over Russia oil links, warns analyst.
content: Ukraine will curb diesel imports from India starting October 1, citing concerns over links to Russian crude oil supplies. Security agencies ordered lab tests on every shipment to detect any Russian-origin fuel components. In August, Ukraine imported 119,000 tons of Indian diesel, about 18% of its total fuel imports. Before the war, Ukraine relied on Belarus and Russia, but now mostly sources diesel from European nations.

title: After Trump-Modi thaw, US trade chief heads to India for key negotiations.
content: After weeks of tension, India and the United States are set to revive stalled trade negotiations following a breakthrough in ties between President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The renewed warmth between the two leaders has paved the way for the resumption of talks, which had earlier been derailed by tariff disputes and disagreements linked to India’s purchase of Russian oil. Brendan Lynch, the American chief negotiator and Assistant US Trade Representative for South and Central Asia, will arrive in New Delhi on Monday night, September 15. He is scheduled to meet India’s chief negotiator, Rajesh Agrawal, on Tuesday. Agrawal confirmed Lynch’s visit and said both sides are keen to “fast-track” the discussions, though he offered no details about the agenda. The visit is being seen as a significant step forward, as trade talks between the two nations were originally planned for late August but were called off amid escalating tensions. Washington had imposed steep tariffs on Indian exports, with the United States arguing that India’s purchase of Russian crude was indirectly financing the Ukraine war. Under the revised trade measures, the US imposed a 25 percent penalty tariff in early August, followed by another 25 percent on August 27. These tariffs had become a major sticking point, straining relations until Trump and Modi’s latest diplomatic thaw helped reopen the door for talks.

title: Shilpa Shetty says Shah Rukh Khan’s iconic rise came from his self-belief: “He saw himself as a King before the world did.
content: On the sets of Super Dancer 5, Shilpa Shetty shared a heartfelt moment while talking about her Baazigar co-star Shah Rukh Khan. The occasion came after a young contestant delivered a spirited performance to Sachi Yeh Kahani Hai from Kabhi Haan Kabhi Naa. Impressed, Shilpa compared the child’s energy to Shah Rukh’s and reflected on what truly makes him “King Khan.” She recalled how Shah Rukh always believed in himself long before the world recognized him, stressing that his unshakable confidence not only shaped his journey but also inspired others to see him the way he saw himself. On Super Dancer 5, Shilpa praised Shah Rukh Khan, recalling how his self-belief set him apart from the start. She said Shah Rukh saw himself as a King before anyone else did, and that faith made the world believe it too. Shilpa highlighted the power of confidence, noting it inspires others to see you exactly as you see yourself. The actress shared the moment after a contestant’s energetic dance reminded her of Shah Rukh’s spirit.

title: “On PM Modi’s 75th Birthday, Rahul Gandhi and BJP Leaders Share Strong Messages of His Journey and Leadership.
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi marked his 75th birthday on Wednesday, September 17, with warm wishes pouring in from across the country. Leaders from the Centre, Opposition, and state governments extended greetings, acknowledging his leadership and contribution to India’s growth. Many lauded his “extraordinary leadership” and vision, crediting him with shaping India’s development and global standing. Political leaders and citizens alike shared messages celebrating his dedication to public service and his ability to inspire collective progress. Alongside the praise, several leaders also wished him continued good health and long life, reflecting the respect he commands beyond party lines. President Murmu said PM Modi fostered a culture of great goals, applauding his vision that inspires the nation toward ambition and progress. Amit Shah hailed him as a symbol of sacrifice and dedication, while Rahul Gandhi extended greetings, wishing the Prime Minister good health.

title: “BJP Accuses Tejashwi Yadav of Insulting PM Modi’s Mother Again During ‘Bihar Adhikar Yatra.
content: BJP has alleged that RJD workers again hurled abuses at PM Modi’s late mother during Tejashwi Yadav’s ‘Bihar Adhikar Yatra’, sparking fresh political controversy in Bihar. Deputy CM Samrat Choudhary accused Tejashwi of encouraging such behaviour, calling it against Bihar’s culture. He warned that women of the state will hold him accountable for the insults. Another deputy CM, Vijay Kumar Sinha, echoed the charges, saying the incident showed the RJD’s mindset. Meanwhile, the video of the episode went viral, though its authenticity remains unverified.

title: “Trump Says Putin ‘Let Him Down,’ Admits He Thought Ending Ukraine War Would Be Easy.
content: Trump admitted he once thought the Ukraine war would be the “easiest” to solve, but said Putin has “really let him down” during a press meet with UK PM Keir Starmer at Chequers. Trump warned Putin over the ongoing war, saying Russian troops are dying at a higher rate than Ukrainians. He added, “War turns out opposite of what you expect.” He urged Europe to cut Russian oil imports, stressing lower prices could weaken Putin’s war. Starmer echoed this, urging tougher energy and defense measures. Starmer said Putin’s latest assaults show he isn’t seeking peace, citing mass killings and NATO airspace violations as proof of Russia’s aggressive stance.

title: “Trump’s $100K H-1B Visa Deadline: What It Means for Indians.
content: Trump’s sudden $100K H-1B visa fee sparked panic, with firms warning Indian workers abroad they could be stranded or denied entry if they didn’t return in time. The White House clarified the fee is a one-time charge for new petitions only, not renewals or existing holders, and Indians on valid H-1B visas don’t need to rush back. Tech giants like Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta had urged employees to stay in the US, causing fear among Indian families, who form the largest group of H-1B beneficiaries. India’s foreign ministry warned of humanitarian impacts. With Indians making up 71% of H-1B visas, the $100K rule could reshape IT hiring and raise costs for US firms.

title: "India slams Pakistan’s ‘deep irony’ after it lectures others on human rights at UNHRC.
content: India strongly rebuked Pakistan at the UN Human Rights Council (UNHRC), calling out its hypocrisy for lecturing others on rights violations while ignoring its own record. Speaking in Geneva at the 34th meeting of the 60th UNHRC session, Indian diplomat Mohammed Hussain said it was “deeply ironic” for Pakistan to spread propaganda instead of addressing the persecution of minorities within its borders. He urged Pakistan to confront the harsh reality of religious and ethnic oppression at home before pointing fingers at others. The criticism came amid fresh turmoil in Pakistan itself, where an explosion at a Taliban-owned compound in the Tirah Valley killed at least 24 people, including women and children. While police said the blast was caused by stored explosives, local residents alleged it was the result of government air strikes. The Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf’s provincial wing claimed that “jet bombing” destroyed several homes, while police officials reported that 14 militants and 10 civilians were among the dead. Outraged locals staged protests, accusing authorities of failing to protect innocent lives. International voices at the UNHRC also underscored Pakistan’s worsening human rights record. Experts highlighted its dismal ranking of 158th on the World Press Freedom Index and a sharp rise in blasphemy-related imprisonments, with over 700 people behind bars in 2025—a 300 percent increase from the previous year. Reports from rights groups detailed hundreds of enforced disappearances and killings in Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, while activists accused Pakistan of carrying out prolonged military operations in these regions. Together, these testimonies painted a damning picture of systemic abuse that Pakistan must face at home rather than deflect abroad.

title: "Why Elon Musk Wants People to Stop Using Netflix.
content: Elon Musk has once again sparked controversy online, this time by urging people to cancel their Netflix subscriptions. On Wednesday, he repeatedly posted on X (formerly Twitter), saying things like “Cancel Netflix” and even “Cancel Netflix for the health of your kids.” These remarks came after Musk himself canceled his own subscription, taking a direct swipe at the streaming giant. The backlash started when Musk reshared a post from another user who claimed they had canceled Netflix because the company employed “someone who celebrated the murder of Charlie Kirk.” Musk appeared to agree with the criticism and doubled down on the call to boycott the platform. The issue stems from comments made by Hamish Steele, the creator of Netflix’s animated series Dead End: Paranormal Park. After conservative activist Charlie Kirk was shot dead, Steele shared a profanity-filled post on Bluesky, calling Kirk a “nazi.” Many people online accused Steele of mocking Kirk’s death and using offensive language, which fueled Musk’s anger against Netflix.

title: Zubeen Garg’s manager and a festival organiser booked for murder: Report.
content: Assam Police have charged Zubeen Garg’s manager Siddharth Sharma and organiser Shyamkanu Mahanta with murder over the singer’s death in Singapore. . The CID is questioning both after a court sent them to 14 days’ custody. Police added Section 103 of BNS, which deals with punishment for murder. Zubeen’s wife Garima said she trusts the probe team and hopes the truth about the singer’s final moments in Singapore will be revealed soon.

title: Abhishek and Aishwarya Rai Bachchan sue Google and YouTube for ₹4 crore over deepfake videos: Report.
content: Abhishek and Aishwarya Rai Bachchan have filed a ₹4 crore lawsuit against Google and YouTube over AI deepfakes misusing their photos and videos. The couple accused the platforms of letting harmful, explicit deepfake clips spread. Some channels hosted 250+ manipulated videos with millions of views. Delhi HC has asked Google to reply by Jan 15. Meanwhile, Abhishek preps for King and Raja Shivaji, while Aishwarya has not confirmed her next film.

title: American in Bengaluru shows off his 5-year Indian visa, Modi says, “Welcome home, bhai.
content: An American man living in Bengaluru went viral after showing off his 5-year Indian visa and praising PM Modi for welcoming foreign talent. He even joked about Trump’s restrictive stance, saying, “Modi says welcome home bhai.” Identified as Tony Klor, he highlighted how India is opening doors for blockchain and AI professionals, sparking positive reactions from many on social media. The visa, valid till 2030 with stays up to 180 days, is meant for foreigners to explore or set up businesses—showing India’s push to attract global talent.

title: Cuttack on High Alert: Internet Suspended, City Under Shutdown.
content: Cuttack is under a 36-hour curfew after Durga Puja clashes turned violent. Internet and social media are blocked to stop rumors and restore peace. Violence began at Haathi Pokhari over loud music, leading to stone-pelting, arson, and police injuries. Several areas remain tense under heavy security. VHP rallies defied orders, sparking fresh clashes, fires, and chaos. Shops were burnt, CCTV destroyed, and police fired tear gas to control mobs. Leaders urged calm, calling communal harmony non-negotiable. Security forces patrol streets as police arrest suspects and track social media rumors.

title: “After 5 Years, Rhea Chakraborty Finally Gets Her Passport Back; Says Patience Kept Her Going.
content: After 5 years, Rhea Chakraborty finally gets her passport back. She calls it a symbol of patience, hope, and a fresh start. Her passport was seized in 2020 during the Sushant Singh Rajput case probe. Since then, she faced endless battles, scrutiny, and silence. Sharing a picture, Rhea wrote: “Patience was my only passport for 5 years. Today I hold it again, ready for Chapter 2. Satyameva Jayate.” With fans cheering her return, Rhea hints at new projects ahead. Her resilience marks a new chapter after years of struggle.

title: LinkedIn CEO Ryan Roslansky says degrees will matter less in the future workplace.
content: LinkedIn CEO Ryan Roslansky believes that college degrees will become less important in the coming years, especially as artificial intelligence reshapes the workplace. He explained that companies are already shifting their priorities away from traditional qualifications and are instead looking for people who can adapt quickly, learn new skills, and work with emerging technologies. Speaking at a fireside chat in LinkedIn’s San Francisco office, Roslansky said the old idea of success being tied to having a top degree from a prestigious university is fading. In his view, what will matter most is whether a person is flexible, forward-thinking, and open to embracing change. Skills such as problem-solving, curiosity, and technological fluency will carry more weight than credentials on paper. Roslansky described this change as one of the most exciting shifts in the world of work. He emphasized that the future belongs to people who are willing to keep learning and evolving, rather than those who rely only on their academic background. He added that this new outlook “opens up the playing field” for a much larger group of people, giving opportunities to anyone ready to grow, regardless of their degree or college background.

title: OpenAI Adds New ‘Chat’ Feature, Turning ChatGPT Into a Mini Operating System.
content: OpenAI’s new Chat feature makes ChatGPT more than a chatbot—it’s now a mini OS. Soon, with an AI device launch, users may rely on it instead of apps. Developers can build mini apps inside ChatGPT, from designing posters to shopping with Instant Checkout—turning it into a full ecosystem like iOS. This move sets OpenAI apart from Google and Microsoft. But privacy, security, and lock-in risks loom as rivals race to own the AI interface layer.

title: “Sitharaman Launches Real-Time Forex Settlement System, Calls for Responsible AI-Driven Fintech Revolution.

title: Shabana Azmi Makes History as First Indian Actor to Lead Acting Masterclass at New York’s Prestigious Lee Strasberg Theatre & Film Institute.
content: The iconic Lee Strasberg Theatre & Film Institute, New York, invited veteran Shabana Azmi for a masterclass, making her the first Indian actor to get this honour. Inspired by her acclaimed solo play Broken Images, the institute’s president Victoria Krane personally reached out to Azmi, marking a historic recognition. Humbled by the honour, Azmi said she takes every accolade as motivation to keep working, adding that if her recognition makes India proud, she feels truly happy.

title: Zubeen Garg's cousin, Assam Police officer, arrested in singer's death case
content: The Assam CID has arrested five people, including Zubeen Garg’s cousin and police officer Sandipan Garg, in connection with the singer’s death in Singapore. Others held include festival organiser Shyamkanu Mahanta, Garg’s manager Siddharth Sharma, bandmate Shekhar Jyoti Goswami, and singer Amritprava Mahanta. CID has added murder charges after interrogation, also probing financial irregularities linked to Mahanta. Central agencies like ED and IT may step in.

title: Delhi High Court Sends Notice to Makers of The Ba**ds of Bollywood* After Sameer Wankhede Challenges Aryan Khan Web Series.
content: Former NCB officer Sameer Wankhede has sued Netflix and Red Chillies over Aryan Khan’s debut series The Ba**ds of Bollywood*. The Delhi High Court has issued notices after Wankhede claimed the show defames him by portraying a cop similar to his 2021 drug bust role. The court earlier dismissed his plea but allowed a revised petition. Wankhede’s lawyer said the series led to trolling of his family online. The satire, directed by Aryan Khan, shows a cop raiding a Bollywood party — a plot many link to Aryan’s own high-profile arrest in 2021. Wankhede argues the show was made to damage his image, while the case raises debate over free expression, satire, and defamation in cinema.

title: China Imposes Tougher Export Curbs on Rare Earths, Raising Global Supply Concerns.
content: China now requires overseas exporters using its rare earths to get licenses, tightening control over a sector central to global tech supply chains. The curbs extend to magnets, extraction tech, and recycling. Military uses are mostly banned, while semiconductor exports need case-by-case approval. Rare earths are critical for defense, autos, and high-tech industries. China supplies 70% of the world’s demand, giving it strong leverage globally. The minerals have long been a flashpoint in the US-China trade war, with Beijing using its dominance to push back against Washington. These new rules come just before Xi and Trump’s expected meeting, raising fears of fresh tensions even as both nations pursue a wider trade deal.

title: Jaishankar Sends Strong Message to Taliban, Says India Has Major Stakes in Afghanistan’s Future.
content: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar met Afghanistan’s Foreign Minister Amir Khan Muttaqi in New Delhi, marking their first high-level talks since 2021. Jaishankar welcomed Muttaqi’s visit, saying India values direct dialogue and stands as a supportive neighbour with deep interest in Afghanistan’s growth and peace. He announced India will reopen its embassy in Kabul, a major diplomatic move to strengthen ties and support Afghanistan’s stability and development. Muttaqi, under UN sanctions, reached New Delhi with special permission after attending a global conference on Afghanistan in Russia.

title: "Raj Kundra Blames Demonetisation for Loan Default Amid Fraud Allegations: Report.
content: A case has been filed against Raj Kundra and Shilpa Shetty for allegedly duping Mumbai businessman Deepak Kothari of nearly ₹60 crore in a loan-cum-investment deal. The deal was linked to their now-defunct platform, Best Deal TV, where the couple served as directors. Kothari informed probe agencies that he couldn’t recover the loan after demonetisation in 2016. He claimed that between 2015 and 2023, the couple used the money meant for business growth for personal expenses. The funds were transferred under a share subscription agreement and a supplementary agreement. The Economic Offences Wing (EOW) of Mumbai Police is investigating the matter. Kothari alleged that the couple promised monthly returns and repayment but failed to honour it. Shilpa Shetty later stepped down as director after insolvency proceedings were initiated against the company. Amid the probe, the Bombay High Court denied the couple’s plea to quash the Look Out Circular (LOC) and barred foreign travel unless they deposit ₹60 crore. The court has adjourned the matter until October 14.

title: Breaking: Ashish Kumar spills on Aryan Khan — “Super calm, never angry… Red Chillies paid before time!.
content: Ashish Kumar, who played the plain clothes cop in The Ba**ds Of Bollywood*, left a strong impression despite appearing in just one scene. In an exclusive chat, he shared how his journey began after sending his photos to casting director Nandini Shrikent. Aryan Khan personally appreciated his audition and explained the role, ensuring every shot was perfect during rehearsals. Ashish revealed that Aryan was extremely humble and never lost his cool, no matter how many retakes were needed. He treated everyone with respect and worked like a true professional. “He talked like a friend and was very chilled out,” Ashish recalled, praising Aryan’s focus on perfection and teamwork on set. Speaking about Red Chillies Entertainment, Ashish praised the production house for its professionalism. “They paid even before the scheduled time and treated me with great respect,” he said, appreciating the team, especially executive producer Bonnie Jain, for their smooth coordination and warm attitude. Before entering acting, Ashish studied engineering in Bhopal but always dreamed of being an actor. From theatre in Bharat Bhavan to workshops with Neeraj Kabi and others, his journey was full of struggle and learning. Despite family pressure and financial hardships, his passion never faded — leading him back to Mumbai in 2021, where he finally landed The Ba**ds Of Bollywood* in 2023.

title: "Dubai-based fitness coach says Indians face the most racism and are deeply misunderstood — post sparks massive reaction online.
content: A Dubai-based fitness coach, Nathan, has gone viral for his heartfelt post defending Indians against racism and stereotypes. Having worked with Indian clients for seven years, Nathan said Indians are often mocked online and labeled unfairly, yet few truly understand their spirit. He described Indians as warm, proud, and full of life, saying their culture and energy shine once you experience their homes, food, and celebrations. Nathan also shared how his Indian clients helped him grow as a trainer, praising their loyalty and saying, “No community supports you like the Indian community once you help them.”

title: Karisma Kapoor’s kids expose shocking truth about Sunjay Kapur’s will
content: Senior advocate Mahesh Jethmalani has alleged that Sunjay Kapur’s will was fabricated and not written by him. He told the court that metadata showed the document was created on another man’s computer. The file was reportedly edited and turned into a PDF just hours before it was shared in a WhatsApp group that included Priya Kapur and others — raising serious questions about its origin. Jethmalani pointed out several errors — wrong addresses, missing assets, and inconsistent spellings — arguing that a man like Sunjay Kapur could never draft something so careless. 🏠📑 The High Court said the case raises major doubts about the will’s authenticity. As the battle continues, it’s not just about inheritance anymore — it’s memory versus metadata. ⚖️🔥

title: India drops to 85th place on the Henley Passport Index, with visa-free travel now limited to 57 countries.
content: India’s passport now ranks 85th in the 2025 Henley Passport Index, allowing visa-free access to 57 countries, down from 59 last year. Singapore tops the list with access to 193 destinations, followed by South Korea and Japan, showing Asia’s strong travel influence. European countries like Germany, Italy, and Spain stay in the top 10, while the US drops out for the first time since the index began. India’s mobility remains moderate, with visa-free entry to 12 nations and visa-on-arrival in 27, while Afghanistan has the world’s weakest passport.India’s passport now ranks 85th in the 2025 Henley Passport Index, allowing visa-free access to 57 countries, down from 59 last year. title: India responds after Trump claims PM Modi promised him Russian oil deal.
content: Former US President Donald Trump claimed that Prime Minister Narendra Modi assured him India would stop buying oil from Russia soon. Speaking to reporters at the White House, Trump said he was “not happy” about India’s Russian oil purchases but added that Modi promised to end them within a short time. In response, India firmly reiterated its stance, saying its energy decisions are guided by the goal of protecting Indian consumers amid global volatility. MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal said India focuses on diversifying energy sources and ensuring stable supplies to meet its growing market needs.

title: Akshay Kumar Wins Legal Shield Against Unauthorized Use of AI-Generated Content.
content: The Bombay HC granted interim relief to Akshay Kumar against unauthorized AI use of his image, voice, and likeness. Justice Doctor noted the misuse harms stature and has serious consequences. The case cited deepfake trailers, AI-generated audio, and fake social accounts misleading the public. The order aligns with a trend of Indian celebrities seeking legal protection to safeguard digital identity and reputation.

title: Zepto raises $450 million ahead of its expected IPO next year.
content: Zepto has raised $450 million in new funding, valuing the company at $7 billion. This round is seen as its last before a possible IPO next year. The funding saw participation from major investors like CalPERS, General Catalyst, Goodwater Capital, and Lightspeed Ventures. Zepto now holds around $900 million in cash. The quick-commerce firm faces tough competition from Swiggy’s Instamart and Eternal’s Blinkit as demand for 10-minute delivery continues to surge in Indian cities. Zepto may push its IPO to 2026 to focus on profitability, but experts say this funding round could be the final boost before it hits the public market. #Zepto #IPO

title: Singapore allows Assam SIT to probe Zubeen Garg’s death, but why did violence erupt during transfer of the accused?
content: Singapore Police have granted permission to the Assam Special Investigation Team (SIT) to visit the country and carry forward the probe into singer Zubeen Garg’s mysterious death. Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma confirmed the development, stating that the SIT would soon travel to Singapore to collect crucial evidence and question individuals connected to the case. Meanwhile, tension gripped parts of Assam as violence broke out during the transfer of the accused in the Zubeen Garg case. Protesters clashed with security forces, prompting authorities to impose prohibitory orders under Section 144 to prevent further unrest. Police said the situation is now under control, but security has been tightened in sensitive areas to maintain law and order. Several people have reportedly been detained in connection with the violence. The state government has urged citizens -he investigation to proceed smoothly. The SIT is expected to coordinate closely with Singapore authorities to ensure a transparent and thorough probe.

title: Gunshots fired again at Kapil Sharma’s café in Canada — why is the Bishnoi gang targeting him repeatedly?
content: Indian entertainer Kapil Sharma’s café in Canada has come under attack for the third time in just four months. Gunshots were fired at the restaurant, raising concerns about the safety of staff and patrons. Authorities confirmed that the incident occurred late at night, and no casualties were reported. Security at the café has been increased following repeated attacks. The Bishnoi gang has claimed responsibility for the latest shooting, adding to fears over organized criminal activities targeting businesses in the area. Police are investigating possible motives and connections. Kapil Sharma and his team have urged the public to stay calm while the authorities handle the situation. Officials are working to ensure strict security and prevent any further incidents.

title: Andhra Pradesh and Odisha Brace for Impact as Powerful Cyclone Montha Set to Hit Today.
content: Cyclone Montha nears the coast, putting Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, and Odisha on high alert for heavy rain and strong winds CM Chandrababu Naidu orders swift action to protect lives, evacuate people, and ensure clean drinking water in coastal areas. IMD warns Montha may turn into a severe storm, hitting between Machilipatnam and Kalingapatnam with winds up to 110 kmph. NDRF and SDRF teams are on standby as states prepare relief, rescue, and medical camps to handle the cyclone’s impact.

title: Breaking News LIVE: Crude Bomb Blast Reported Outside Grameen Bank Headquarters in Bangladesh — Security on High Alert
content: New Delhi | November 10, 2025:A high-intensity car bomb explosion near Red Fort Metro Station (Lal Qila) on Monday evening has killed at least eight people and injured several others, according to officials. The National Investigation Agency (NIA) and Delhi Police Special Cell have cordoned off the area and launched a joint investigation. The blast occurred near Gate No. 1 of Red Fort Metro Station, triggering a massive fire that engulfed nearby vehicles and shattered glass windows in the vicinity. Thick plumes of smoke were seen rising above the heritage site, causing panic among locals and tourists. At least seven fire tenders were rushed to the spot to douse the flames. The Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL) and anti-terror squads are currently examining the debris to determine the composition of the explosive. Officials confirmed that the explosion happened just hours after police seized 360 kg of suspected ammonium nitrate and a cache of arms from a Faridabad residence linked to a Kashmiri academic, raising fears of a possible terror link. Security across Delhi has been tightened, with NIA teams scanning CCTV footage and traffic movement near the Red Fort area.

title: Katrina Kaif Discharged from Hospital, Heads Home with Newborn Baby Boy; Vicky Kaushal and Family Celebrate Joyous Arrival
content: Mumbai, November 10: Bollywood power couple Katrina Kaif and Vicky Kaushal have officially stepped into parenthood as they welcomed their baby boy on November 7, 2025. After spending a few days under medical observation at HN Reliance Hospital, Katrina was discharged on November 10 and was seen heading home with her newborn. According to visuals shared by Zoom Chronicle, Katrina was spotted leaving the hospital in a car, accompanied by her husband Vicky Kaushal and their baby boy. The heart warming moment marked the couple’s first public appearance since becoming parents. Industry and Family Reactions As soon as the news broke, social media was flooded with congratulatory messages from friends and fans across the film fraternity. Karan Johar took to Instagram stories to extend his warm wishes, calling parenthood “a magical world of endless love.” Meanwhile, Kareena Kapoor Khan welcomed Katrina to the “boy mom club.” Sham Kaushal’s Emotional Message Vicky Kaushal’s father, Sham Kaushal, shared an emotional note on social media, expressing his gratitude to God. He wrote, “Shukriya Rab da... Kal se Bhagwan ka mere parivar pe itne meherban rehne ke liye. Hum sab bahut khush hain aur bahut blessed feel kar rahe hain. May God’s blessings always remain on my children and the youngest Kaushal. So so happy on becoming Dada. Rab Rakha.” Sunny Kaushal Becomes the Coolest Chacha Adding to the family joy, Sunny Kaushal also expressed his excitement by posting, “Main chacha ban gaya!” — a simple yet heart-touching declaration of his happiness. Fans and well-wishers continue to shower love and blessings on the new parents as they begin this beautiful new chapter together.

title: Bihar Exit Polls 2025 Highlights: NDA Projected to Retain Power with 145+ Seats; Prashant Kishore Factor Shakes Opposition – Zoom Chronicle Report
content: Patna, November 12 (Zoom Chronicle News Report):The Bihar Assembly Election 2025 has concluded, and the exit polls paint a decisive picture of the state’s political mood. According to an aggregate of nine major exit polls, the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) — comprising the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP), Janata Dal (United), and Lok Janshakti Party (Ram Vilas) led by Chirag Paswan — is projected to win over 145 seats in the 243-member Bihar Assembly. The Opposition Mahagathbandhan, led by RJD’s Tejashwi Yadav, Congress, and Left parties, appears to be trailing far behind, with predictions ranging between 98 to 110 seats, indicating a strong anti-incumbency resistance that failed to consolidate. 🔹 NDA Maintains Lead Despite Challenges The BJP-JDU-Chirag Paswan alliance seems to have effectively managed caste equations, welfare messaging, and Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s popularity to maintain its dominance. The Nitish Kumar-led JD(U), despite facing criticism over unemployment and infrastructure issues, appears to have benefited from BJP’s strong urban and youth support base. Political analysts suggest that the Chirag Paswan factor — once seen as a vote-splitter — turned into an advantage for the NDA in crucial constituencies, particularly in central and western Bihar. 🔹 Prashant Kishore Factor Shakes Up the Equation The Prashant Kishore-led Jan Suraaj movement, contesting its first assembly polls, has reportedly made a visible impact in several regions, including Champaran, Saran, and Muzaffarpur. While Kishore’s party may not win many seats, analysts believe it could influence vote shares and reshape Bihar’s political narrative ahead of the 2029 Lok Sabha elections. 🔹 Exit Poll Trends Summary NDA (BJP+JD(U)+LJP): 130-145 seats Mahagathbandhan (RJD+INC+Left): 98-110 seats Others (Including Jan Suraaj, AIMIM, Independents): 2-7 seats If these projections hold true, Nitish Kumar is likely to return as Chief Minister for another term, backed by the BJP’s national support. 🔹 Final Verdict on Counting Day The final results will be declared on November 15, 2025, when Bihar’s electorate will officially decide whether the NDA maintains its stronghold or if a surprise awaits in the counting booths.

title: Andhra Pradesh Emerges as India’s Next Industrial Powerhouse Ahead of CII Partnership Summit 2025
content: As Visakhapatnam prepares to host the 30th CII Partnership Summit 2025 on November 14–15 at AU Engineering College Grounds, all eyes are on Andhra Pradesh, the rising star of India’s industrial landscape. With 44,000 acres of land earmarked for industrial development, a massive green hydrogen hub, and world-class connectivity projects, the State offers an unbeatable package for investors. According to industry experts, the essential factors for industrial growth—land, power, water, transport, manpower, and raw materials—are abundantly available in Andhra Pradesh. The Andhra Pradesh Industrial Infrastructure Corporation (APIIC) has already designated land across 20 districts, with Tirupati, Anakapalli, and Kadapa leading in availability. Strategically, over 92% of this land lies near major National Industrial Corridors—Bengaluru-Hyderabad, Chennai-Bengaluru, and Chennai-Visakhapatnam. The upcoming NTPC Green Hydrogen Hub near Visakhapatnam, launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in January 2025, is set to make Andhra Pradesh a leader in clean energy. Expected to be operational by 2032, it will produce 1,500 TPD of green hydrogen and 7,500 TPD of derivatives, including green methanol and sustainable aviation fuel. Connectivity is another key strength. The Bhogapuram International Airport, South Coast Railway Zone, and four major ports, including Visakhapatnam Port Trust, will boost trade and logistics. The State is also rich in mineral wealth, housing the world’s largest barite deposit at Mangampeta and vast reserves of limestone, bauxite, and rare earth elements. The Jonnagiri gold mine in Kurnool adds to its economic allure, capable of producing 750 kg of gold annually, according to an SBI research report. With strategic planning, green energy leadership, and abundant natural resources, Andhra Pradesh is poised to become India’s next industrial and investment hub.

title: All MoUs at CII Summit Signed With Serious Investors: CM Chandrababu Naidu
content: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu reaffirmed that every Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) signed during the 30th CII Partnership Summit was with investors who have shown genuine intent and long-term commitment toward the State’s growth. Speaking at the closing press conference of the two-day summit, the Chief Minister said the new coalition government has revived the business environment after a difficult period between 2019 and 2024, when investment sentiment was low and many industries moved out of the State. Following the keynote address by Hon’ble Minister Kondapalli Srinivas, the summit convened a high-level panel featuring distinguished leaders from industry and global institutions. The panel comprised Mr. Thomas John Muthoot, Chairman of the CII Southern Region and Chairman & Managing Director of Muthoot Fincorp Ltd.; Mr. M. Ponnuswami, Co-Chairman of the CII National MSME Council and CMD, Pon Pure Chemical India Pvt. Ltd.; Mr. Philipp Assmus, CEO and Founder, FyrstGen, Switzerland; Mr. Prasad Maganti, Founder and CEO, Maganti IT, United States; Mr. Tammireddy Siva Sankara Rao, Chairman, Andhra Pradesh MSME Development Corporation; Mr. Ritin Koria, Project Specialist, United Nations Industrial Development Organization (UNIDO); and Mr. Ameya Prabhu, Co-Chairman, CII International Council and Founder & CEO, NAFA Capital Advisors Pvt.
